project.name: ecc-universal
project.description: Everything Claude Code (ECC) — a harness-native agent operating system distributed as a Claude Code plugin/marketplace. Markdown-dominant: 271 skills, 67 agents, 92 commands, 114 per-language rules, plus Node.js install/CI automation, a Rust control plane (ecc2/), a Python multi-provider LLM layer (src/), and cross-harness MCP conventions.

nodes.name: auto-tmux-dev.js
nodes.summary: PreToolUse hook that intercepts dev-server commands (like `npm run dev`) and automatically redirects them to run inside a tmux window, preventing the agent from blocking on long-running processes.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Main hook entry point that parses stdin JSON, detects dev-server command patterns, and rewrites the command to spawn in tmux, returning an allow/modify result.

nodes.name: bash-hook-dispatcher.js
nodes.summary: Central dispatcher that routes incoming PreBash and PostBash hook events through a composed pipeline of individual hook modules, aggregating their outputs and forwarding the final result to Claude Code.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a reduce-style chain where each hook's output feeds into the next; normalizeHookResult bridges between legacy spawn-based hooks and modern module-based hooks.

nodes.name: runHooks
nodes.summary: Iterates over a list of hook modules in sequence, checking each is enabled, running it, normalizing the result, and accumulating additionalContext for Claude.

nodes.name: normalizeHookResult
nodes.summary: Normalizes the raw output of a hook (Buffer, object, or string) into a canonical result object with exitCode, stderr, and additionalContext fields.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point: reads stdin, dispatches to either runPreBash or runPostBash based on the hook type argument, and writes the result to stdout.

nodes.name: readStdinRaw
nodes.summary: Reads all stdin bytes asynchronously and resolves a Promise with the raw string.

nodes.name: block-no-verify.js
nodes.summary: PreBash hook that statically analyzes shell commands to detect and block any `git commit --no-verify` or equivalent flag combinations that would bypass commit hooks.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements a hand-rolled shell tokenizer rather than using a shell parser library, handling quoting, escaping, and comment detection to achieve robust detection without exec.

nodes.name: tokenizeShellWords
nodes.summary: Splits a raw shell string into tokens while respecting single-quote, double-quote, and backslash escaping rules.

nodes.name: detectGitCommand
nodes.summary: Locates a `git` invocation in a shell command string and identifies its subcommand and position, handling multi-command chains and comment prefixes.

nodes.name: hasNoVerifyFlag
nodes.summary: Given a tokenized commit command segment, checks whether `--no-verify` or its short equivalent `-n` is present in a position that would skip hooks.

nodes.name: hasHooksPathOverride
nodes.summary: Detects use of --hooks-path pointing to /dev/null or empty directories as a mechanism to silence hooks.

nodes.name: checkCommand
nodes.summary: Orchestrates detection of a git commit with hook-bypass flags, returning a block result with an explanatory message if found.

nodes.name: extractCommand
nodes.summary: Extracts the raw shell command string from either a JSON hook payload or a plain string input.

nodes.name: findGit
nodes.summary: Finds the byte offset of the git binary in a shell command, ensuring it is a valid command token and not part of a comment.

nodes.name: check-hook-enabled.js
nodes.summary: Tiny utility script that reads a hook ID from process arguments and exits non-zero if that hook is disabled via `ECC_DISABLED_HOOKS`, used for shell-level gating checks.

nodes.name: doc-file-warning.js
nodes.summary: PreWrite hook that detects when Claude Code is about to write an ad-hoc documentation file outside sanctioned directories and injects a visible warning into the tool's additional context.

nodes.name: gateguard-fact-force.js
nodes.summary: The main GateGuard hook — a multi-mode PreToolUse/PreBash gate that blocks or prompts-for-facts before destructive Bash commands, file edits/writes to sensitive paths, and cross-session state tracking to enforce a per-session denial budget.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses atomic-write rename semantics (write to temp then rename) for state persistence and SHA-256 hashing of session/file keys to keep state file small and collision-resistant.

nodes.name: isDestructiveGit
nodes.summary: Classifies a tokenized git command as destructive (e.g., reset --hard, push --force, checkout --, clean -f) using a comprehensive set of subcommand-aware rules.

nodes.name: isDestructiveFindExec
nodes.summary: Checks whether a find -exec chain executes a destructive command (rm, git reset, etc.) as its terminal action.

nodes.name: isDestructiveBash
nodes.summary: Top-level predicate that combines rm-rf, destructive git, SQL DDL, and find-exec checks to determine whether a shell command is destructive.

nodes.name: saveState
nodes.summary: Persists gate state to disk using atomic rename to avoid corrupt reads under concurrent invocations, merging denial counts from any concurrent writes.

nodes.name: isReadOnlyGitIntrospection
nodes.summary: Returns true when a git command is purely introspective (status, log, diff, show, etc.) with no flags that would mutate state, used to allowlist safe git calls.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Main gate dispatcher that classifies the incoming tool event (Edit/Write/Bash), checks state, applies the denial budget, and returns either an allow or deny-with-message result.

nodes.name: tokenizeAllowlistedShellWords
nodes.summary: Shell word tokenizer that only returns tokens from the ECC allowlisted command set, used for safe introspection checks.

nodes.name: loadState
nodes.summary: Reads and validates the per-session GateGuard state file, returning a fresh default state if missing or expired.

nodes.name: collectExecutableBodies
nodes.summary: Recursively extracts all command substitution bodies, subshell groups, and brace groups from a shell command for destructive-command scanning.

nodes.name: post-bash-build-complete.js
nodes.summary: PostBash hook that detects when a build command (npm/pnpm/yarn run build) completes and injects a success notification into the tool's additional context.

nodes.name: post-bash-command-log.js
nodes.summary: PostBash hook that appends a sanitized record of every executed shell command (with credentials redacted) to a persistent log file in the user's home directory.

nodes.name: post-bash-dispatcher.js
nodes.summary: Thin entry-point script for the PostBash hook that delegates to bash-hook-dispatcher.js, acting as the registered hook binary in settings.json.

nodes.name: post-bash-pr-created.js
nodes.summary: PostBash hook that detects when `gh pr create` completes, extracts the PR URL and number from output, and surfaces a formatted review reminder in the additional context.

nodes.name: pre-bash-commit-quality.js
nodes.summary: PreBash hook that intercepts `git commit` commands to validate staged file content for console.log/debugger statements, TODO markers, secret patterns, and conventional commit message format, blocking commits that fail checks.

nodes.name: findFileIssues
nodes.summary: Scans staged file content for forbidden patterns (console.log, debugger, credential-like tokens, blank TODO comments) and returns a list of human-readable issue strings.

nodes.name: validateCommitMessage
nodes.summary: Parses the commit message from a git commit command string and validates it against conventional commit format, length limits, and capitalization rules.

nodes.name: runLinter
nodes.summary: Runs ESLint and markdownlint on staged files if the tools are present, collecting and returning their combined output.

nodes.name: evaluate
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full commit-quality evaluation: parses the command, checks for staged files, runs file-issue checks, validates the commit message, invokes linters, and composes the final block-or-allow result.

nodes.name: resolveCommand
nodes.summary: Resolves a command name to its absolute executable path by searching PATH directories and accounting for Windows file extensions.

nodes.name: pre-bash-dev-server-block.js
nodes.summary: PreBash hook that blocks commands which would start a foreground dev server (e.g., `npm run dev`, `vite`, `next dev`), preventing the agent from hanging on interactive processes.

nodes.name: readToken
nodes.summary: Reads a single shell token from a command string starting at a given index, handling quoted strings, escape sequences, and redirections.

nodes.name: getLeadingCommandWord
nodes.summary: Extracts the first meaningful command word from a shell segment, skipping env-var assignments, option flags, and known prefix words like `sudo`, `env`, `npx`.

nodes.name: collectCheckSegments
nodes.summary: Recursively extracts all shell segments (including command substitutions and subshells) that need to be checked for dev-server patterns.

nodes.name: pre-bash-dispatcher.js
nodes.summary: Thin entry-point script for the PreBash hook that delegates to bash-hook-dispatcher.js, acting as the registered hook binary in settings.json.

nodes.name: pre-bash-git-push-reminder.js
nodes.summary: PreBash hook that detects `git push` commands and injects a reminder into the additional context prompting the agent to confirm it has reviewed the diff first.

nodes.name: pre-bash-tmux-reminder.js
nodes.summary: PreBash hook that detects long-running commands (install, test, build, docker, etc.) and injects a reminder to use tmux so the agent doesn't block the main session.

nodes.name: pre-write-doc-warn.js
nodes.summary: Ultra-thin PreWrite hook entry point that delegates directly to doc-file-warning.js's run function.

nodes.name: pretooluse-visible-output.js
nodes.summary: Shared utility module that provides helper functions for building and combining the `additionalContext` field in PreToolUse hook responses, used by multiple hooks to surface visible messages to the agent.

nodes.name: run-with-flags.js
nodes.summary: Universal hook wrapper that loads a target hook module by script path (from CLI args), applies ECC_DISABLED_HOOKS and ECC_HOOK_PROFILE gating, supports dry-run mode, and falls back to spawning legacy hooks as child processes.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports both CommonJS module-style hooks (exports a `run` function) and legacy script-style hooks (invoked via spawnSync), bridging old and new hook formats.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that reads stdin, resolves the hook script path, checks hook-enabled state, optionally dry-runs, then dispatches to module or legacy-spawn execution path.

nodes.name: resolveHookResult
nodes.summary: Normalizes a hook module's return value (Buffer, string, or result object) into a canonical stdout string and exit code.

nodes.name: extractTargetContext
nodes.summary: Parses the hook stdin payload to extract the tool name, file path, and command for use in dry-run preview messages.

nodes.name: buildDryRunPreview
nodes.summary: Builds a human-readable dry-run summary string showing which hook would fire and what target it would act on.

nodes.name: resolveLegacySpawnStdout
nodes.summary: Applies echo-sanitization and exit-code extraction to the raw stdout of a spawned legacy hook process.

nodes.name: hook-flags.js
nodes.summary: Shared library that reads and parses the ECC_HOOK_PROFILE and ECC_DISABLED_HOOKS environment variables, exposing `isHookEnabled` and `isDryRun` utilities consumed by all hook dispatchers.

nodes.name: isHookEnabled
nodes.summary: Returns true when a hook ID is not in the disabled list and its allowed profiles include the current active profile.

nodes.name: getDisabledHookIds
nodes.summary: Parses the ECC_DISABLED_HOOKS comma-separated environment variable into a normalized Set of hook ID strings.

nodes.name: parseProfiles
nodes.summary: Converts an array or CSV string of profile names into a filtered array of valid profile values.

nodes.name: shell-split.js
nodes.summary: Library that splits a raw shell command string into top-level command segments delimited by `;`, `|`, `&&`, `||`, handling quoted strings and subshell groups to avoid splitting inside them.

nodes.name: splitShellSegments
nodes.summary: Character-by-character shell command splitter that correctly handles single quotes, double quotes, backtick subshells, and parenthetical subshells when splitting on operators.

nodes.name: shell-substitution.js
nodes.summary: Library that extracts nested command substitution bodies (`$(...)`, backtick), subshell groups (`(...)`), and brace groups (`{...}`) from shell commands for recursive destructive-command checking.
nodes.languageNotes: All three extraction functions use recursive self-call to handle arbitrarily nested subshells, with depth tracking to prevent runaway recursion.

nodes.name: extractCommandSubstitutions
nodes.summary: Extracts the body strings of all `$(...)` and backtick command substitutions in a shell string, recursing into nested substitutions.

nodes.name: extractSubshellGroups
nodes.summary: Extracts bodies of all subshell groups `(...)` in a shell string, handling nested parens and quoted strings.

nodes.name: extractBraceGroups
nodes.summary: Extracts bodies of all brace command groups `{...}` in a shell string, distinguishing command groups from parameter expansion braces.

nodes.name: hook-flags.test.js
nodes.summary: Test suite for scripts/lib/hook-flags.js covering normalizeId, getHookProfile, getDisabledHookIds, parseProfiles, and isHookEnabled across numerous environment variable combinations.

nodes.name: runTests
nodes.summary: Defines and executes the full hook-flags test suite, covering all exported functions with positive and negative cases.

nodes.name: pre-bash-commit-quality.test.js
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for pre-bash-commit-quality.js that sets up temporary git repos, stages files, and asserts that the hook correctly blocks or allows commits with various code quality issues.

nodes.name: inTempRepo
nodes.summary: Test helper that creates a fresh temp git repository, changes into it for the test callback, and cleans up afterward.

nodes.name: captureConsoleError
nodes.summary: Monkey-patches console.error to capture lines emitted by the hook under test and restore the original afterward.

nodes.name: withEnv
nodes.summary: Sets environment variable overrides for the duration of a test callback, restoring original values afterward.

nodes.name: shell-split.test.js
nodes.summary: Unit test suite for scripts/lib/shell-split.js verifying that splitShellSegments correctly segments shell commands across a range of operator and quoting scenarios.

nodes.name: isSuspiciousDocPath
nodes.summary: Returns true when a file path looks like an ad-hoc Markdown or text file being written outside structured documentation directories.

nodes.name: sanitizeCommand
nodes.summary: Strips tokens matching credential patterns (AWS keys, GitHub tokens, passwords, Authorization headers) from a command string before logging.

nodes.name: combineAdditionalContext
nodes.summary: Merges two additionalContext strings (current and new) into a single newline-separated string, normalizing each.

nodes.name: buildPreToolUseAdditionalContext
nodes.summary: Wraps a context value into the JSON structure expected by Claude Code's PreToolUse additionalContext field.

nodes.name: auto-update.js
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that auto-discovers and re-runs install-apply for all ECC plugins that have an existing install state, enabling bulk in-place updates across multiple install targets.

nodes.name: catalog.js
nodes.summary: CLI tool for browsing the ECC install catalog — lists available install profiles and components, and displays details for a specific component by ID.

nodes.name: merge-mcp-config.js
nodes.summary: Codex utility that idempotently merges ECC-managed MCP server entries into a TOML MCP config file, adding new entries and removing stale ones while preserving user-added sections.

nodes.name: consult.js
nodes.summary: CLI consultation engine that ranks ECC install components and profiles against a free-text query using TF-style token scoring, returning the best-matching install suggestions.

nodes.name: doctor.js
nodes.summary: CLI diagnostic tool that analyzes installed ECC state for drift, missing files, and integrity issues, reporting a human-readable or JSON health report.

nodes.name: ecc.js
nodes.summary: Top-level ECC CLI dispatcher that maps subcommands (install, update, uninstall, doctor, etc.) to their respective scripts via child process execution.

nodes.name: install-apply.js
nodes.summary: CLI front-end for the install-apply phase: parses request arguments, resolves an install plan, and delegates execution to the install executor.

nodes.name: install-plan.js
nodes.summary: CLI tool for previewing the install plan without applying it, showing which profiles, modules, and components would be installed for a given request.

nodes.name: cursor-agent-names.js
nodes.summary: Small utility providing filename and path conventions for Cursor agent definition files derived from a component ID.

nodes.name: install-executor.js
nodes.summary: Core installation engine that builds and materializes install plans for all supported targets (Claude, Cursor, Antigravity), handling file copies, JSON merges, scaffold creation, and manifest-driven installs.

nodes.name: install-lifecycle.js
nodes.summary: Lifecycle manager for ECC installations providing discovery, health analysis, repair, and uninstall operations over persisted install state records.
nodes.languageNotes: At 1226 lines and 37 functions this is the largest file in the batch; it is the authoritative source for install health semantics, repair logic, and uninstall teardown.

nodes.name: install-manifests.js
nodes.summary: Manifest loader and catalog resolver that reads the ECC install manifests, exposes install profiles/modules/components, and resolves a full install plan from a normalized request.

nodes.name: install-state.js
nodes.summary: Persistence layer for ECC install state: creates, reads, validates, and writes the JSON install-state file that tracks what has been installed and where.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains a large hand-written fallback JSON schema validator (createFallbackValidator, lines 50-219) used when a compiled schema is unavailable.

nodes.name: apply.js
nodes.summary: Install-phase applier that materializes a resolved install plan by writing files, merging JSON, and wiring Claude hook paths with plugin-root placeholder substitution.

nodes.name: config.js
nodes.summary: Install configuration loader that resolves and validates the per-project `.ecc-install.json` config file, applying defaults and returning a parsed configuration object.

nodes.name: request.js
nodes.summary: Install request parser and normalizer that converts CLI arguments into a canonical install request structure with resolved profile, module, component, and target selections.

nodes.name: runtime.js
nodes.summary: Runtime bridge that creates an install plan from a normalized request by delegating to the install executor's plan-creation function.

nodes.name: mcp-config.js
nodes.summary: MCP configuration utilities for parsing disabled-server lists and filtering an MCP config object to remove disabled servers by name.

nodes.name: list-installed.js
nodes.summary: CLI tool that discovers and lists all ECC installations found across configured targets, displaying their install status in human-readable or JSON format.

nodes.name: runAutoUpdate
nodes.summary: Discovers all installed ECC states, validates their repo roots, and sequentially re-applies each via the install-apply CLI, collecting per-record results.

nodes.name: resolveCommand
nodes.summary: Maps a CLI subcommand name and arguments to a concrete script path and argument list for child-process execution.

nodes.name: runCommand
nodes.summary: Executes a resolved ECC subcommand as a child process, forwarding stdio and propagating the exit code.

nodes.name: rankComponents
nodes.summary: Scores all available install components against the query tokens and returns a ranked list with install command suggestions.

nodes.name: buildConsultation
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full consultation response by tokenizing the query, ranking components and profiles, and assembling the JSON payload.

nodes.name: createManifestInstallPlan
nodes.summary: Builds a full file-operation install plan from a resolved manifest install request, handling component modules, JSON merges, and hook bootstrap registration.

nodes.name: planCursorLegacyInstall
nodes.summary: Generates file copy and scaffold operations for a Cursor legacy install, including agent files and MCP config merging.

nodes.name: planAntigravityLegacyInstall
nodes.summary: Generates file copy operations for an Antigravity legacy install target, resolving locale-specific content and module boundaries.

nodes.name: createLegacyInstallPlan
nodes.summary: Entry point for legacy install plan creation; dispatches to target-specific plan builders (Claude, Cursor, Antigravity) based on the install target.

nodes.name: executeUninstallOperation
nodes.summary: Executes a single uninstall operation — file deletion, JSON key removal, or hook deregistration — with state-aware rollback guards.

nodes.name: inspectManagedOperation
nodes.summary: Inspects a single managed install operation to determine its current health status (ok, drifted, missing, conflicted) by comparing on-disk state to recorded expectations.

nodes.name: analyzeRecord
nodes.summary: Performs deep analysis of an install record by inspecting each managed operation and aggregating individual operation statuses into a record-level health summary.

nodes.name: repairInstalledStates
nodes.summary: Discovers all installed states and executes repair plans for any that report drift or missing files, restoring them to expected content.

nodes.name: uninstallInstalledStates
nodes.summary: Discovers and removes all installed ECC states across targets, executing uninstall operations and cleaning up empty parent directories.

nodes.name: buildDoctorReport
nodes.summary: Assembles a full doctor report by discovering all installed states and analyzing each for health issues.

nodes.name: resolveInstallPlan
nodes.summary: Resolves a normalized install request into a fully-expanded install plan by expanding profiles to modules, modules to components, and applying exclusion and locale rules.

nodes.name: loadInstallManifests
nodes.summary: Reads and merges the ECC install manifest JSON files from the package source root, caching the result for subsequent calls.

nodes.name: createFallbackValidator
nodes.summary: Hand-written JSON schema validator for the install state document used as a fallback when a compiled AJV validator is unavailable.

nodes.name: createInstallState
nodes.summary: Constructs a new install state record from source metadata and an executed install plan, recording all operations for future health checks.

nodes.name: applyInstallPlan
nodes.summary: Executes each operation in an install plan — copying files, merging JSON configs, and writing Claude hooks — materializing the installation on disk.

nodes.name: parseInstallArgs
nodes.summary: Parses raw CLI arguments into a structured install request object, recognizing flags for target, profile, module, component, and locale selections.

nodes.name: normalizeInstallRequest
nodes.summary: Normalizes a parsed install request by resolving defaults, deduplicating selections, and validating the target and component IDs.

nodes.name: createInstallPlanFromRequest
nodes.summary: Bridges a normalized install request to the executor's manifest install plan builder, returning the complete plan object.

nodes.name: filterMcpConfig
nodes.summary: Filters an MCP configuration object, removing server entries whose names appear in the disabled-servers set.

nodes.name: check-console-log.js
nodes.summary: PreToolUse hook that scans pending tool input for console.log statements and blocks or warns when found, preserving pass-through behavior on non-critical errors.

nodes.name: cursor-session-env.js
nodes.summary: SessionStart hook for Cursor IDE that resolves and injects the ECC agent data home path via environment variables and additional context, supporting Cursor-specific session persistence.

nodes.name: desktop-notify.js
nodes.summary: Cross-platform desktop notification hook that fires native OS alerts (macOS osascript, Windows PowerShell) when a Claude session completes, with TTY detection and tmux/screen awareness.

nodes.name: findTerminalTTY
nodes.summary: Walks up the process tree via `ps` to find the controlling TTY of the Claude process, used to route macOS notifications correctly when under a terminal multiplexer.

nodes.name: notifyMacOS
nodes.summary: Sends a macOS desktop notification via osascript, handling TTY detection, tmux/screen multiplexer routing, and string escaping.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Main entry point that parses hook input JSON, extracts a summary, dispatches to platform-specific notifiers, and handles abort/cancellation reasons.

nodes.name: evaluate-session.js
nodes.summary: PostSession hook that evaluates whether a session produced enough content to warrant saving, counting learned-skill entries and exiting early when the threshold is not met.

nodes.name: plugin-hook-bootstrap.js
nodes.summary: Core hook dispatcher that reads stdin, resolves the target hook script path within a plugin root, and spawns it as either a Node.js process or a shell script, forwarding stdin/stdout and propagating exit codes.

nodes.name: spawnNode
nodes.summary: Spawns a target hook script as a Node.js child process, ensuring AGENT_DATA_HOME is set in the environment and forwarding raw stdin.

nodes.name: spawnShell
nodes.summary: Spawns a target hook script via a discovered shell binary, setting the agent data home environment and piping stdin/stdout.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates hook dispatch: reads raw stdin, resolves the plugin root, selects Node.js or shell spawner based on file extension, and handles errors with passthrough fallback.

nodes.name: post-edit-console-warn.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse hook that emits a warning message to stderr when an Edit or Write tool call leaves console.log statements in the modified file.

nodes.name: pre-compact.js
nodes.summary: PreCompact hook that logs a compaction event timestamp and lists recent session files to the session log directory before Claude compacts the context window.

nodes.name: session-activity-tracker.js
nodes.summary: High-complexity PostToolUse hook that captures a rich activity row for every tool call — sanitizing parameters, extracting file paths and events, building diff/patch previews from git, and appending JSONL records to the session activity log.

nodes.name: redactSecrets
nodes.summary: Sanitizes a string value by replacing common secret patterns (tokens, AWS keys, GitHub personal access tokens, passwords) with <REDACTED>.

nodes.name: buildActivityRow
nodes.summary: Assembles the full activity record for a single tool-call event, combining hook event metadata, session ID, file events, file paths, input/output summaries, and a unique timestamp-based ID.

nodes.name: enrichFileEventFromWorkingTree
nodes.summary: Augments a file event with a real git diff/patch preview by locating the git repo root, finding candidate paths, and calling git diff on tracked files.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Parses the raw hook JSON input, builds an activity row, and appends it as a JSONL record to the session activity log file.

nodes.name: session-end-marker.js
nodes.summary: Stop hook that records a session-end marker by removing the session lease and stopping the observer process when no other active sessions remain for the same project context.

nodes.name: session-end.js
nodes.summary: Stop hook that extracts a session summary from the Claude transcript, builds a structured Markdown session file with metadata header, and writes or updates it in the ECC sessions directory.

nodes.name: extractSessionSummary
nodes.summary: Parses the Claude JSONL transcript file to extract user messages, tool calls used, and files modified, returning a structured summary object.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the session-end workflow: resolves paths, extracts summary from the transcript, merges or creates the Markdown session file, and writes the final result.

nodes.name: session-start.js
nodes.summary: SessionStart hook that injects rich additional context into each new Claude session: recent session history, learned skills summary, active instincts, and session aliases — while pruning expired sessions and registering the new session lease.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Core session-start logic: prunes expired sessions, registers a session lease, assembles context from instincts/learned skills/aliases/prior sessions, and writes the session-start payload to stdout.

nodes.name: summarizeActiveInstincts
nodes.summary: Reads instinct files from both project and global homunculus directories, deduplicates them, ranks by confidence, and formats a concise text block for the session context.

nodes.name: summarizeLearnedSkills
nodes.summary: Collects learned skill files, generates per-file summaries, and concatenates them into a formatted context block listing available learned skills.

nodes.name: selectMatchingSession
nodes.summary: Finds the most recent session file that matches the current working directory and project name by reading Markdown session headers.

nodes.name: suggest-compact.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse hook that monitors tool-call count and context window usage, suggesting or nudging the user to compact when configurable thresholds are exceeded.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Main suggest-compact logic: increments tool-call counter, reads context window state, evaluates thresholds, and writes a compact suggestion to stdout when triggered.

nodes.name: incrementToolCallCount
nodes.summary: Reads, increments, and persists a per-session tool-call counter file, returning the updated count.

nodes.name: agent-data-home.js
nodes.summary: Library module that resolves the ECC agent data home directory across environments (Cursor IDE, generic Claude, project overrides), reading project config files and falling back to sensible OS defaults.

nodes.name: resolveAgentDataHome
nodes.summary: Resolves the agent data home path by checking environment variable, Cursor runtime detection, project config, and OS-default fallback in priority order.

nodes.name: ensureAgentDataHomeEnv
nodes.summary: Sets the AGENT_DATA_HOME environment variable if not already present, used by hook spawners to propagate the resolved path to child processes.

nodes.name: getCursorSessionEnvPayload
nodes.summary: Builds the environment variable payload for Cursor session initialization, including the agent data home path and optional Cursor-default flag.

nodes.name: observer-sessions.js
nodes.summary: Library that manages observer session state: resolves project context from git remotes, handles session lease files for multi-agent tracking, and controls the observer daemon lifecycle.

nodes.name: resolveProjectContext
nodes.summary: Resolves a project context object containing the project ID, root path, and display name by combining git remote URL, worktree path, and project config.

nodes.name: stopObserverForContext
nodes.summary: Sends a stop signal to the observer daemon for a given project context by writing to the signal counter file and removing the PID file.

nodes.name: package-manager.js
nodes.summary: Library that detects, configures, and retrieves run/exec commands for the active Node.js package manager (npm, pnpm, yarn, bun), persisting preferences per-project and globally.

nodes.name: getPackageManager
nodes.summary: Resolves the active package manager for the current project by checking env var, project config, lock file detection, package.json, and availability fallback.

nodes.name: getCommandPattern
nodes.summary: Builds a regex or string pattern matching the given command for the detected package manager, used to identify and replace package manager invocations in scripts.

nodes.name: project-detect.js
nodes.summary: Library that detects the primary technology stack of the current project by inspecting lock files, config files, package.json dependencies, and language-specific manifest files.

nodes.name: detectProjectType
nodes.summary: Top-level detection function that returns a structured project type descriptor by walking through language/framework heuristics (Node, Python, Go, Rust, PHP, Elixir).

nodes.name: session-aliases.js
nodes.summary: Library managing named session aliases stored as a JSON file, providing CRUD operations (set, get, list, delete, rename) and cleanup of aliases pointing to stale session files.

nodes.name: saveAliases
nodes.summary: Atomically saves the aliases map to disk, enforcing uniqueness constraints on alias names and session IDs before writing.

nodes.name: cleanupAliases
nodes.summary: Removes stale aliases that reference session files no longer present on disk, keeping the aliases store consistent.

nodes.name: dashboard.js
nodes.summary: Skill evolution dashboard renderer that produces an ASCII terminal report with sparkline success rates, failure clusters, amendment timelines, and version history panels.

nodes.name: renderDashboard
nodes.summary: Assembles the full skill evolution dashboard by composing all panel renderers into a formatted terminal report string.

nodes.name: renderSuccessRatePanel
nodes.summary: Renders the success-rate panel showing per-skill sparklines and trend arrows for daily execution counts and success percentages.

nodes.name: health.js
nodes.summary: Skill health analysis library that scans skill execution records to compute success rates, failure trends, pending amendments, and last-run timestamps per skill.

nodes.name: collectSkillHealth
nodes.summary: Main health collection function that discovers all skills, loads their execution records, computes health metrics (success rate, failure trend, pending amendments), and returns a health report.

nodes.name: discoverSkills
nodes.summary: Scans configured skill roots to discover all skill definition files, returning a list of skill identifiers with their paths.

nodes.name: index.js
nodes.summary: Barrel entry point for the skill-evolution module, re-exporting all sub-modules: dashboard, health, provenance, tracker, and versioning.

nodes.name: provenance.js
nodes.summary: Provides skill provenance tracking: resolves skill root directories, classifies skill paths by source, validates provenance records, and reads/writes provenance metadata for each installed skill.

nodes.name: tracker.js
nodes.summary: Records and retrieves skill execution history by writing normalized execution records to JSONL files, enabling usage tracking for the skill evolution system.

nodes.name: versioning.js
nodes.summary: Manages skill versioning lifecycle: creates snapshots of skill files, reads evolution logs, lists version history, and supports rolling back skills to a previous version.

nodes.name: transcript-context.js
nodes.summary: Reads Claude conversation transcript files to extract token usage, resolve context-window size, and compute context-bucket thresholds used by hooks such as the suggest-compact hook.

nodes.name: utils.js
nodes.summary: Core shared utility library providing path resolution, file I/O, git helpers, stdin JSON parsing, process execution, and logging used throughout all ECC scripts and hooks.

nodes.name: setup-package-manager.js
nodes.summary: CLI script that detects, lists, and configures the ECC package manager at the global or project scope, guiding users through choosing between available package sources.

nodes.name: skills-health.js
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for skill health reporting; delegates to the skill-evolution dashboard and health modules to display diagnostics and evolution status for installed skills.

nodes.name: classifySkillPath
nodes.summary: Classifies a skill path as belonging to the project root, user home, or an external source, used for provenance attribution.

nodes.name: validateProvenance
nodes.summary: Validates a provenance record object's required fields and timestamp format, throwing on invalid data.

nodes.name: readProvenance
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the provenance JSON file for a given skill directory, returning null if not found.

nodes.name: writeProvenance
nodes.summary: Serializes and writes a provenance record to the skill's provenance JSON file, validating before write.

nodes.name: normalizeExecutionRecord
nodes.summary: Normalizes raw execution data into a canonical execution record shape, coercing numeric fields and defaulting missing values.

nodes.name: recordSkillExecution
nodes.summary: Appends a normalized execution record to the skill's JSONL runs file, creating the file and its parent directories if needed.

nodes.name: readSkillExecutionRecords
nodes.summary: Reads all execution records for a given skill from its JSONL runs file.

nodes.name: ensureSkillVersioning
nodes.summary: Initializes versioning for a skill by creating the versions directory and taking the first version snapshot if one does not already exist.

nodes.name: listVersions
nodes.summary: Lists all saved versions of a skill sorted by version number, reading each version's metadata.

nodes.name: createVersion
nodes.summary: Creates a new versioned snapshot of a skill's content, incrementing the version counter and appending an evolution log entry.

nodes.name: rollbackTo
nodes.summary: Restores a skill to a specified previous version by overwriting the current skill file with the snapshot content and recording the rollback in the evolution log.

nodes.name: readLatestContextTokens
nodes.summary: Scans the tail of the most recent transcript JSONL file to find the latest token usage entry, returning both usage and context-window fields.

nodes.name: resolveContextWindowTokens
nodes.summary: Resolves the total context window size for the current session from transcript data or environment overrides.

nodes.name: resolveContextThreshold
nodes.summary: Computes the alert threshold token count from environment variables or a default fraction of the context window size.

nodes.name: computeContextBucket
nodes.summary: Bins a token usage value into a named context-fullness bucket (e.g., low/medium/high/critical) for reporting and hook triggers.

nodes.name: findFiles
nodes.summary: Recursively searches directories for files matching given glob patterns with optional exclusions, returning sorted absolute paths.

nodes.name: readStdinJson
nodes.summary: Reads and parses JSON from stdin with timeout and error handling, used by hooks to receive event data from the Claude Code harness.

nodes.name: runCommand
nodes.summary: Executes a shell command with environment overrides and returns stdout, stderr, and exit code, wrapping Node's child_process.spawnSync.

nodes.name: getGitModifiedFiles
nodes.summary: Returns a list of files modified in the git working tree, optionally filtered by status flags (staged, unstaged, untracked).

nodes.name: sanitizeSessionId
nodes.summary: Sanitizes a raw session ID string to a safe filesystem-compatible format, stripping disallowed characters.

nodes.name: replaceInFile
nodes.summary: Performs in-place string replacement in a file, reading, modifying, and writing the file contents.

nodes.name: detectAndShow
nodes.summary: Auto-detects the current ECC package manager configuration and displays a summary of active settings for both global and project scopes.

nodes.name: setGlobal
nodes.summary: Sets the global-scope ECC package manager by writing the configuration to the user's global Claude settings.

nodes.name: setProject
nodes.summary: Sets the project-scoped ECC package manager by writing the configuration to the project's local Claude settings.

nodes.summary: Main entry point for the skills-health CLI, parsing arguments and delegating to health or dashboard report generation.

nodes.summary: Master test runner containing all ECC hook integration tests organized into suites for session lifecycle, cost-tracking, suggest-compact, and post-edit behaviors.

nodes.summary: Test helper that executes an ECC hook script as a subprocess with injected stdin JSON, returning its output and exit code.

nodes.summary: Master test runner for the session-aliases library, exercising alias CRUD operations and persistence under various session isolation conditions.

nodes.summary: Comprehensive master test runner for the utils library, covering all helper functions including path resolution, file I/O, git utilities, and string manipulation.

nodes.summary: Orchestrates uninstall flow: parses args, invokes the uninstall lifecycle, and prints or JSON-outputs results.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package root for the src/llm Python LLM abstraction library; re-exports the public API surface (interface types, providers, tools) and exposes a convenience `gui` entry point that launches the interactive CLI selector.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: Python `__main__` module enabling `python -m llm` invocation; delegates directly to the CLI selector's `main` function.

nodes.name: selector.py
nodes.summary: Interactive terminal CLI for selecting an LLM provider and model; handles user prompts, validates choices, and persists the selection to a config file.

nodes.name: interactive_select
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full interactive provider-and-model selection flow, coordinating provider listing, model listing, user input prompts, and optional config persistence.

nodes.name: save_config
nodes.summary: Writes the selected provider and model to a dotenv-style config file, with optional persistence toggle.

nodes.name: interface.py
nodes.summary: Defines the abstract `LLMProvider` base class and the full error hierarchy (`LLMError`, `AuthenticationError`, `RateLimitError`, `ContextLengthError`, `ModelNotFoundError`, `ToolExecutionError`) that all provider implementations must implement and raise.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python ABC protocol with abstract methods; error hierarchy is single-inheritance from LLMError which itself extends Exception.

nodes.name: LLMProvider
nodes.summary: Abstract base class defining the contract for all LLM provider implementations: generate, list_models, validate_config, supports_tools, supports_vision, and get_default_model.

nodes.name: types.py
nodes.summary: Central type definitions for the LLM abstraction layer: enums (`Role`, `ProviderType`), dataclasses for message/tool structures (`Message`, `ToolDefinition`, `ToolCall`, `ToolResult`), and the primary LLM I/O contracts (`LLMInput`, `LLMOutput`, `ModelInfo`).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python dataclasses with `field(default_factory=...)` for mutable defaults; ToolDefinition includes both `to_openai_tool` and `to_anthropic_tool` serialization helpers for multi-provider compatibility.

nodes.name: LLMInput
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a fully-formed LLM request: messages, model, temperature, max_tokens, tool definitions, streaming flag, and arbitrary metadata.

nodes.name: LLMOutput
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a normalized LLM response: content, tool_calls, model used, token usage stats, stop reason, and metadata.

nodes.name: ToolDefinition
nodes.summary: Dataclass for describing a tool/function callable by the LLM; provides `to_openai_tool` and `to_anthropic_tool` serialization methods for cross-provider compatibility.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the prompt sub-package; re-exports `PromptBuilder`, `PromptConfig`, `adapt_messages_for_provider`, and template registry utilities.

nodes.name: builder.py
nodes.summary: Provides `PromptBuilder` for assembling structured message lists from system/user templates and tool definitions, plus `adapt_messages_for_provider` to apply provider-specific formatting transformations.

nodes.name: PromptBuilder
nodes.summary: Assembles a final list of `Message` objects from templates, system context, user inputs, and tool definitions, applying provider-specific formatting for system prompts and tool descriptions.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: In-memory prompt template registry providing `register_template`, `deregister_template`, `get_template`, and `get_template_or_default` for managing named prompt templates at runtime.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the providers sub-package; re-exports all concrete provider classes (`ClaudeProvider`, `OpenAIProvider`, `OllamaProvider`, `AstraflowProvider`, `AstraflowCNProvider`) and the resolver utilities.

nodes.name: astraflow.py
nodes.summary: Implements the Astraflow LLM provider (and its China-region variant) by delegating to an OpenAI-compatible API endpoint; includes `_AstraflowBaseProvider` with configurable env-var-driven API key, base URL, and model resolution.

nodes.name: _AstraflowBaseProvider
nodes.summary: Abstract base for Astraflow providers; reads API credentials and model from environment variables, wraps the OpenAI client, and normalizes LLMOutput including tool call extraction and error mapping.

nodes.name: claude.py
nodes.summary: Concrete `ClaudeProvider` implementing the `LLMProvider` interface via the Anthropic SDK; handles Anthropic-specific message formatting, tool call extraction from content blocks, and error mapping to the standard error hierarchy.

nodes.name: ClaudeProvider
nodes.summary: Full Anthropic/Claude LLM provider with model catalog, Anthropic-native tool formatting, content block parsing for text and tool_use blocks, and normalized error handling.

nodes.name: constants.py
nodes.summary: Minimal constants file holding shared values (e.g., default API endpoint base URL) used across provider implementations.

nodes.name: ollama.py
nodes.summary: Concrete `OllamaProvider` implementing the `LLMProvider` interface for local Ollama models; communicates via direct HTTP calls to the Ollama REST API and normalizes the response into `LLMOutput`.

nodes.name: OllamaProvider
nodes.summary: Local Ollama LLM provider using urllib HTTP calls (no SDK dependency); normalizes Ollama chat completion responses to LLMOutput including tool call handling and standard error mapping.

nodes.name: openai.py
nodes.summary: Concrete `OpenAIProvider` implementing the `LLMProvider` interface via the openai Python SDK; handles message formatting, tool call JSON parsing, and error mapping to the standard error hierarchy.

nodes.name: OpenAIProvider
nodes.summary: Full OpenAI LLM provider supporting multiple GPT-4 model variants; uses the openai SDK for completions, normalizes tool calls from function call format, and maps API errors to the common hierarchy.

nodes.name: resolver.py
nodes.summary: Resolves and instantiates the correct `LLMProvider` at runtime by reading provider type from environment variables or a saved `.env`-style config file; also exposes `register_provider` for extending the provider map.

nodes.name: get_provider
nodes.summary: Factory function that resolves provider type from config/env, looks up the corresponding provider class, and returns an instantiated `LLMProvider`.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the tools sub-package; re-exports `ToolRegistry`, `ToolExecutor`, and `ReActAgent` from executor.py.

nodes.name: executor.py
nodes.summary: Implements the tool execution layer: `ToolRegistry` for registering callable functions, `ToolExecutor` for dispatching tool calls from LLM responses, and `ReActAgent` for multi-turn Reason+Act agentic loops.

nodes.name: ReActAgent
nodes.summary: Implements a ReAct (Reason+Act) agentic loop: sends LLM requests, handles tool calls by executing them via ToolExecutor, and iterates until the LLM produces a final response or the maximum iteration limit is reached.

nodes.name: ToolExecutor
nodes.summary: Dispatches tool calls from LLM responses by looking up registered functions in a ToolRegistry, executing them, and wrapping results in normalized ToolResult objects.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the inter-session communication layer for the ECC control plane, including TaskPriority and MessageType enums, message serialization, and helpers for previewing and routing messages between agent sessions.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust module barrel for the comms subsystem; exports enums and free functions for inter-agent messaging via serde_json.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Central configuration module for the ECC2 control plane, defining all runtime config structs (agent profiles, harness runners, orchestration templates, memory connectors, TUI layout), plus TOML loading, merging, and persistence logic.
nodes.languageNotes: Large Rust config module using serde/TOML with multi-level merging and template variable interpolation.

nodes.name: main.rs
nodes.summary: Main entry point and CLI command handler for the ECC2 control-plane binary; implements the full CLI surface (sessions, schedules, remote dispatch, worktrees, memory graph, migration, observability export) via clap subcommands.
nodes.languageNotes: Extremely large Rust binary crate root (~12K lines) combining CLI parsing, memory-graph sync, legacy migration, OTLP export, and coordination logic.

nodes.name: notifications.rs
nodes.summary: Implements desktop and webhook notification subsystems for ECC2, including quiet-hours filtering, notification throttling, webhook payload construction, and shell-command-based desktop notification delivery.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-contained Rust module with no intra-project imports; exports notification config structs and notifier implementations used by config and dashboard.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Provides the tool-call risk assessment engine and observability event types for ECC2, computing risk scores based on tool type, file sensitivity, blast radius, and irreversibility.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust module combining structured event types with a multi-factor risk scoring algorithm for Claude Code tool calls.

nodes.name: daemon.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the background daemon loop for ECC2 session lifecycle management, handling automatic session dispatch, heartbeat enforcement, worktree auto-merge/prune, conflict resolution, and crash recovery.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust async daemon that drives all background automation policies; uses testable recorder pattern for side-effect capture.

nodes.name: manager.rs
nodes.summary: Core session management layer for ECC2, providing session creation, scheduling, remote dispatch, orchestration template execution, budget enforcement, conflict resolution, worktree operations, and agent process spawning.
nodes.languageNotes: The largest single-responsibility module in the control plane (~8K lines); orchestrates all active session lifecycle operations over SQLite state.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Session module barrel that re-exports all session subsystems (daemon, manager, output, runtime, store) and defines core session domain types including SessionState, HarnessKind, RemoteDispatchRequest, and ContextObservationPriority.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust session module root combining pub use re-exports with rich domain-model type definitions for the entire session subsystem.

nodes.name: output.rs
nodes.summary: Provides in-memory session output buffering with OutputStream, OutputLine, and SessionOutputStore types, supporting real-time line accumulation and retrieval for TUI display.
nodes.languageNotes: Small but foundational Rust module providing the output buffer types consumed by both the daemon and TUI dashboard.

nodes.name: runtime.rs
nodes.summary: Implements async session runtime utilities including a DbWriter for background database persistence, command output capture, and stream processing for ECC2 session processes.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust async runtime module using tokio for concurrent DB write batching and stdout/stderr capture.

nodes.name: store.rs
nodes.summary: SQLite-backed state store for the ECC2 control plane, implementing the full persistence layer for sessions, messages, scheduled tasks, remote dispatch requests, worktrees, memory graph entities, tool logs, and conflict incidents.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest persistence module (~7K lines) using rusqlite; handles schema migrations, multi-table queries, context graph compaction, and board metadata derivation.

nodes.name: app.rs
nodes.summary: TUI application bootstrap for ECC2, initializing the terminal, launching the main dashboard render loop, and managing terminal cleanup on exit.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust ratatui TUI bootstrap; thin wrapper that sets up crossterm raw mode and hands control to the Dashboard.

nodes.name: dashboard.rs
nodes.summary: Full-featured terminal UI dashboard for the ECC2 control plane, implementing the complete interactive session monitor with multi-pane layout, session management actions, output streaming, git integration, context graph display, and notification delivery.
nodes.languageNotes: Extremely large Rust ratatui component (~15K lines) that is effectively the entire TUI application; owns all rendering, input handling, and background sync logic.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Minimal barrel module for the ECC2 TUI subsystem, re-exporting app, dashboard, and widgets sub-modules.
nodes.languageNotes: Three-line Rust mod.rs; purely structural, no logic.

nodes.name: widgets.rs
nodes.summary: Custom ratatui widget helpers for ECC2 TUI, providing budget state visualization, token/currency formatting, gradient color computation, and styled line components used across the dashboard.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust ratatui widget library with RGB gradient interpolation and a BudgetState enum driving color-coded budget indicators.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Git worktree management module for ECC2, implementing creation, removal, diff/status queries, patch application, hunk-level staging, commit/PR operations, merge readiness assessment, shared-dependency sync, and conflict detection.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust git worktree abstraction (~2.6K lines) using subprocess git invocations; covers the complete lifecycle from creation through merge and pruning.

nodes.name: Config
nodes.summary: Primary runtime configuration struct for ECC2, aggregating all subsystem settings and providing load, save, and resolution methods for agent profiles, orchestration templates, and harness runners.

nodes.name: PaneNavigationConfig
nodes.summary: Configures keyboard shortcuts for TUI pane navigation, mapping key bindings to focus and movement actions.

nodes.name: BudgetAlertThresholds
nodes.summary: Defines the three-tier budget alert threshold configuration (advisory, warning, critical) with sanitization logic.

nodes.name: Commands
nodes.summary: Top-level clap CLI command enum for the ECC2 binary, routing to session, message, schedule, remote, migration, and graph subcommands.

nodes.name: DesktopNotificationConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration struct for desktop notifications with enabled flag, quiet-hours, and allowed event-type filtering.

nodes.name: WebhookNotificationConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration and dispatch logic for webhook-based notifications, supporting multiple target URLs with event filtering and allow-rules.

nodes.name: DesktopNotifier
nodes.summary: Runtime notifier that checks quiet-hours and dispatches desktop notifications via system commands.

nodes.name: WebhookNotifier
nodes.summary: Runtime notifier that dispatches structured JSON payloads to configured webhook URLs over HTTP.

nodes.name: ToolCallEvent
nodes.summary: Structured event type capturing a tool call invocation with risk score, file paths, and metadata for observability and risk gate enforcement.

nodes.name: HarnessKind
nodes.summary: Enum identifying the AI agent harness type (ClaudeCode, Codex, OpenCode, etc.) for session management and process spawning.

nodes.name: SessionState
nodes.summary: State machine enum for session lifecycle (Pending, Running, Paused, Completed, Failed, etc.) with valid-transition guard logic.

nodes.name: RemoteDispatchRequest
nodes.summary: Represents a pending remote dispatch task request, including task description, target agent profile, and scheduling metadata.

nodes.name: SessionOutputStore
nodes.summary: In-memory output buffer mapping session IDs to their accumulated output lines for real-time TUI display.

nodes.name: OutputStream
nodes.summary: Ordered collection of output lines for a single session, supporting line appending and bounded retrieval.

nodes.name: DbWriter
nodes.summary: Async background writer that batches output-line persistence to the SQLite store, decoupling session output capture from synchronous DB writes.

nodes.name: StateStore
nodes.summary: Central SQLite-backed state store for all ECC2 persistent data, providing typed CRUD and query methods for sessions, messages, schedules, worktrees, context graph, and tool logs.

nodes.name: DaemonActivity
nodes.summary: Tracks daemon background-loop activity counters (dispatch, recovery, rebalance, merge, prune passes) for health monitoring.

nodes.name: Dashboard
nodes.summary: Main TUI application struct for ECC2, managing all interactive session monitor state including pane layout, selection, output cache, search, notifications, and background data sync.

nodes.name: Pane
nodes.summary: Enum of focusable TUI pane slots (Sessions, Output, Metrics, Log) with navigation and collapse logic.

nodes.name: SessionCompletionSummary
nodes.summary: Captures and summarizes a completed session's output, decisions, and warnings for popup display and webhook delivery.

nodes.name: BudgetState
nodes.summary: Enum representing budget consumption levels (Normal, Advisory, Warning, Critical) used to drive color-coded TUI budget indicators.

nodes.name: send
nodes.summary: Sends a typed inter-session message by serializing to JSON and writing to the state store.

nodes.name: preview
nodes.summary: Generates a human-readable preview string for a message, formatting task handoffs with priority, file counts, and truncated content.

nodes.name: load
nodes.summary: Loads ECC2 configuration from the default global and project-specific TOML paths, merging layered overrides.

nodes.name: resolve_orchestration_template
nodes.summary: Resolves a named orchestration template by name, interpolating variable substitutions in task strings and merging step-level overrides.

nodes.name: resolve_agent_profile
nodes.summary: Resolves an agent profile by name following the inheritance chain, returning a fully-merged ResolvedAgentProfile.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Async main entry point that parses CLI args, loads config, initializes the state store, and dispatches to the appropriate command handler.

nodes.name: run_coordination_loop
nodes.summary: Runs the background coordination polling loop, invoking the session daemon to process dispatch backlogs, heartbeats, and worktree automation.

nodes.name: sync_all_memory_connectors
nodes.summary: Synchronizes all configured memory connectors (JSONL, Markdown, dotenv) into the context graph, updating entity observations from external sources.

nodes.name: build_otel_export
nodes.summary: Builds an OpenTelemetry OTLP export payload from session records for external observability pipeline ingestion.

nodes.name: compute_risk
nodes.summary: Computes a composite risk score for a tool call by combining base tool risk, file sensitivity, blast radius, and irreversibility factors.

nodes.name: log
nodes.summary: Persists a tool call event to the state store for later query and risk enforcement.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Main daemon loop entry point that continuously polls the session manager for dispatch, heartbeat, rebalance, merge, and prune operations.

nodes.name: coordinate_backlog_cycle
nodes.summary: Executes one backlog coordination cycle, routing pending handoffs and dispatch requests to available sessions or spawning new ones.

nodes.name: create_session
nodes.summary: Creates and launches a new agent session by resolving the profile, provisioning a worktree if needed, and spawning the harness process.

nodes.name: launch_orchestration_template
nodes.summary: Launches a multi-step orchestration template by resolving variables and sequentially creating sessions for each step.

nodes.name: enforce_budget_hard_limits
nodes.summary: Checks all running sessions against their configured USD and token budgets, pausing or stopping those that have exceeded their limits.

nodes.name: merge_session_worktree
nodes.summary: Merges a completed session's git worktree back into the base branch and cleans up the worktree directory.

nodes.name: init_schema
nodes.summary: Initializes the SQLite database schema, creating all tables and indexes required for the ECC2 state store, and runs any pending migrations.

nodes.name: recall_context_entities
nodes.summary: Performs fuzzy term-based recall across the context graph, returning ranked entities matching the query terms.

nodes.name: compact_context_graph_observations
nodes.summary: Compacts the context graph by removing redundant or superseded observations, keeping the graph queryable and performant.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the TUI terminal session, enters raw mode, creates the dashboard, and runs the event loop until the user quits.

nodes.name: render
nodes.summary: Renders the complete dashboard frame by composing pane areas and dispatching to specialized render methods for sessions, output, metrics, and log panes.

nodes.name: tick
nodes.summary: Processes a single event-loop tick, polling the store for state changes, syncing metrics, and delivering notifications.

nodes.name: sync_from_store
nodes.summary: Pulls fresh session state, messages, worktree health, and context graph data from the state store into dashboard cache.

nodes.name: create_for_session_in_repo
nodes.summary: Creates a git worktree branch for a session in the given repository, setting up the isolated development environment.

nodes.name: merge_into_base
nodes.summary: Merges a worktree branch into the base branch using git, handling fast-forward and merge commit strategies.

nodes.name: merge_readiness
nodes.summary: Assesses whether a worktree branch is ready to be merged by checking for conflicts, divergence, and CI status.

nodes.name: health
nodes.summary: Returns the health status of a worktree, checking for git errors, unresolved conflicts, and staleness.

nodes.name: orchestration-session.js
nodes.summary: Core library for reading tmux orchestration session state: parses worker status files, lists tmux panes, and assembles a snapshot of a multi-worker coordination session. Used by the dmux-tmux adapter to build canonical session data.

nodes.name: buildSessionSnapshot
nodes.summary: Assembles a session snapshot by combining worker file snapshots with live tmux pane data, returning session state, worker count, and per-worker details.

nodes.name: collectSessionSnapshot
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point that resolves a target path or session name, collects tmux pane data, and returns a full session snapshot with repo root and target type metadata.

nodes.name: listTmuxPanes
nodes.summary: Queries tmux via spawnSync to list all panes in a named session, parsing pane title, command, PID, and activity timestamps from the tmux format string.

nodes.name: loadWorkerSnapshots
nodes.summary: Reads all worker directories under a coordination directory and loads their status, task, and handoff text files into worker snapshot objects.

nodes.name: parseWorkerStatus
nodes.summary: Parses a worker's status markdown document into a structured object with state, health, branch, worktree, and runtime fields.

nodes.name: resolveSnapshotTarget
nodes.summary: Resolves a target path (plan JSON file or bare session name) into a canonical session name, coordination directory path, and target type.

nodes.name: canonical-session.js
nodes.summary: Defines the canonical ECC session snapshot schema (ecc.session.v1), validation logic, normalization functions for each adapter type (dmux, claude-history, codex, opencode), and persistence to the state store or a fallback JSON recording file.

nodes.name: validateCanonicalSnapshot
nodes.summary: Validates a canonical session snapshot object against the ecc.session.v1 schema, throwing descriptive errors for any missing or incorrectly typed fields.

nodes.name: persistCanonicalSnapshot
nodes.summary: Persists a canonical session snapshot to the state store (when available) or writes a fallback JSON recording file, validating the snapshot before writing.

nodes.name: normalizeDmuxSnapshot
nodes.summary: Transforms a raw dmux/tmux orchestration snapshot into the canonical ecc.session.v1 format, deriving worker health and aggregate counts.

nodes.name: normalizeClaudeHistorySession
nodes.summary: Transforms a Claude session history record into the canonical ecc.session.v1 format, mapping session metadata to the worker model.

nodes.name: normalizeCodexWorktreeSession
nodes.summary: Transforms a Codex worktree rollout session object into the canonical ecc.session.v1 format with git branch and objective metadata.

nodes.name: normalizeOpencodeSession
nodes.summary: Transforms an Opencode session info object into the canonical ecc.session.v1 format with git branch, model, and objective metadata.

nodes.name: loadStateStore
nodes.summary: Loads a state store instance from the provided factory or options, returning null if unavailable, used to choose between state-store and fallback file persistence.

nodes.name: claude-history.js
nodes.summary: Session adapter for reading Claude Code's own session history files (.tmp), supporting target resolution by explicit prefix, session alias, file path, and latest-session fallback.

nodes.name: createClaudeHistoryAdapter
nodes.summary: Factory that returns a session adapter object with canOpen and open methods for resolving and normalizing Claude history session targets.

nodes.name: resolveSessionRecord
nodes.summary: Resolves a session target string to a session record, supporting explicit prefix targets, session aliases, file paths, and latest-session fallback.

nodes.name: codex-worktree.js
nodes.summary: Session adapter for reading Codex agent worktree rollout files, parsing JSONL message streams to derive session objective, model, timestamps, and git branch metadata.

nodes.name: createCodexWorktreeAdapter
nodes.summary: Factory that returns a session adapter for Codex worktree rollout files, with canOpen and open methods that parse rollout JSONL into canonical session snapshots.

nodes.name: parseCodexRollout
nodes.summary: Parses a Codex rollout JSONL file into a structured session record with objective, model, timestamps, and git branch information.

nodes.name: resolveRolloutPath
nodes.summary: Resolves a target string to the absolute path of a Codex rollout file, handling explicit file targets, ID-based lookup, and latest-rollout fallback.

nodes.name: dmux-tmux.js
nodes.summary: Session adapter for tmux/dmux orchestration sessions, reading live pane state from tmux and persisting a canonical snapshot. Accepts plan JSON files or bare session names as targets.

nodes.name: createDmuxTmuxAdapter
nodes.summary: Factory that returns a dmux-tmux adapter object with canOpen and open methods for collecting live tmux orchestration snapshots and normalizing them to the canonical format.

nodes.name: opencode.js
nodes.summary: Session adapter for Opencode agent sessions, reading session info JSON files from the Opencode storage directory to derive session metadata, model, objective, and git branch.

nodes.name: createOpencodeAdapter
nodes.summary: Factory that returns an Opencode session adapter with canOpen and open methods for resolving and normalizing Opencode session info files to canonical snapshots.

nodes.name: parseOpencodeSession
nodes.summary: Reads an Opencode session info file and its associated message files to derive a structured session record with model, objective, timestamps, and git branch.

nodes.name: resolveSessionInfoPath
nodes.summary: Resolves a target string to the absolute path of an Opencode session info file, supporting explicit file paths, ID-based lookup, and latest-session fallback.

nodes.name: registry.js
nodes.summary: Session adapter registry that instantiates all built-in adapters (claude-history, dmux-tmux, codex-worktree, opencode), routes targets to the correct adapter, and exposes a unified inspectSessionTarget entry point.

nodes.name: createAdapterRegistry
nodes.summary: Creates an adapter registry that can iterate all registered adapters to find one that can handle a given target, then open and snapshot it.

nodes.name: normalizeStructuredTarget
nodes.summary: Normalizes a target that may be a plain string or a structured object with type/value fields and optional context overrides, returning a canonical target/context pair.

nodes.name: session-manager.js
nodes.summary: Core session CRUD library for Claude Code sessions stored as .tmp markdown files; provides listing, searching by date or ID, reading metadata, writing, appending, and deleting session records.

nodes.name: parseSessionFilename
nodes.summary: Parses a session .tmp filename to extract date and short ID, with strict calendar validation to reject impossible dates like Feb 31.

nodes.name: getSessionCandidates
nodes.summary: Scans session search directories for matching .tmp files, applying optional date and search filters, and returns parsed session candidate objects.

nodes.name: parseSessionMetadata
nodes.summary: Parses the YAML-like metadata header embedded in a session .tmp file, extracting title, tags, model, context, and other structured fields.

nodes.name: getMatchingSessionCandidates
nodes.summary: Finds session candidates matching a given ID string, supporting exact match, prefix match, and date-scoped lookup across all session search directories.

nodes.name: getAllSessions
nodes.summary: Returns all session records from all search directories, optionally filtered by date or search term, with full metadata parsed for each session.

nodes.name: getSessionById
nodes.summary: Looks up a single session by ID string, returning null when not found or throwing when multiple sessions match the same ambiguous ID.

nodes.name: orchestration-status.js
nodes.summary: CLI script that accepts a tmux session name or plan JSON path and prints the canonical session snapshot as JSON, with an optional --write flag to save to a file.

nodes.name: orchestration-session.test.js
nodes.summary: Test suite for the orchestration-session library, covering snapshot parsing and tmux pane list behavior.

nodes.name: session-adapters-codex.test.js
nodes.summary: Test suite for the Codex worktree session adapter, verifying rollout file resolution, parsing, and canonical snapshot normalization.

nodes.name: session-adapters-opencode.test.js
nodes.summary: Test suite for the Opencode session adapter, verifying session info file resolution, parsing, and canonical snapshot normalization.

nodes.name: session-adapters.test.js
nodes.summary: Comprehensive test suite for all session adapters (canonical, claude-history, dmux-tmux, registry), covering snapshot normalization, validation, and adapter routing.

nodes.name: session-manager.test.js
nodes.summary: Very large test suite for the session-manager library covering all CRUD operations, filename parsing, metadata extraction, search, and edge cases across date formats and ID matching.

nodes.name: session-inspect.test.js
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for the session-inspect CLI script, exercising target routing through the adapter registry with various session target formats.

nodes.name: antigravity-project.js
nodes.summary: Implements the ECC install-target adapter for the Antigravity AI coding tool, defining which source paths are supported and planning flat-rule file copy operations into the Antigravity project directory.

nodes.name: claude-home.js
nodes.summary: Implements the install-target adapter for Claude's home (~/.claude) installation scope, computing destination paths for skills, agents, rules, and commands with remapping logic for platform-specific source prefixes.

nodes.name: claude-project.js
nodes.summary: Implements the install-target adapter for Claude project-scoped installation (./.claude/), remapping ECC source paths to the project-local Claude config directory with foreign-platform path filtering.

nodes.name: codebuddy-project.js
nodes.summary: Implements a thin install-target adapter for the CodeBuddy IDE, planning flat-rule copy operations and a scaffold operation into the CodeBuddy project rules directory.

nodes.name: codex-home.js
nodes.summary: Minimal stub adapter for the OpenAI Codex home installation target, delegating all behavior to the shared helpers module.

nodes.name: cursor-project.js
nodes.summary: Implements the most complex install-target adapter for the Cursor IDE, handling JSON rule merging, unique-operation deduplication, flat-file and flat-rule operations, and agent name assignment in the .cursor/rules directory.
nodes.languageNotes: Cursor rules require JSON merging and deduplication by destination path — the adapter sorts entries by priority to ensure deterministic outputs.

nodes.name: gemini-project.js
nodes.summary: Minimal stub adapter for the Google Gemini project installation target, delegating all behavior to the shared helpers module.

nodes.name: helpers.js
nodes.summary: Core shared utility library for all install-target adapters, providing path normalization, foreign-platform filtering, managed-operation factories, flat-rule/file copy logic, validation, and the `createInstallTargetAdapter` factory that wires all adapter callbacks into a frozen object.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Object.freeze on the returned adapter to prevent accidental mutation after creation; all adapters are built via this single factory pattern.

nodes.name: joycode-project.js
nodes.summary: Implements a thin install-target adapter for the JoyCode editor, planning flat-rule copy operations and a scaffold operation into the JoyCode project rules directory.

nodes.name: opencode-home.js
nodes.summary: Implements the install-target adapter for OpenCode home installation, including validation that required compiled artefacts (binary and SSE handler) exist before proceeding with the scaffold.

nodes.name: qwen-home.js
nodes.summary: Minimal stub adapter for the Qwen AI home installation target, delegating all behavior to the shared helpers module.

nodes.name: registry.js
nodes.summary: Central registry for all install-target adapters, exporting `listInstallTargetAdapters`, `getInstallTargetAdapter`, and `planInstallTargetScaffold` — the orchestration entry point that validates input, resolves the right adapter, and returns the full operation plan.

nodes.name: zed-project.js
nodes.summary: Implements a thin install-target adapter for the Zed editor, planning flat-rule copy operations and a scaffold operation into the Zed project rules directory.

nodes.name: getClaudeManagedDestinationPath
nodes.summary: Computes the destination path for a given ECC source file within the Claude home directory, remapping skills, agents, rules, and command prefixes to their Claude-specific locations under ~/.claude.

nodes.name: getClaudeManagedDestinationPath
nodes.summary: Computes the destination path for a given ECC source file within the Claude project directory (./.claude/), remapping source prefixes to their project-local Claude config locations.

nodes.name: readJsonObject
nodes.summary: Reads and parses a JSON file from disk, returning a plain object; throws a descriptive error if the file is missing, unreadable, or does not contain an object (e.g., is an array).

nodes.name: createJsonMergeOperation
nodes.summary: Constructs a managed operation object that merges a source JSON file into a Cursor IDE settings file, reading both current and source JSON to produce a merged payload.

nodes.name: isForeignPlatformPath
nodes.summary: Returns true if a source-relative path begins with a platform-specific prefix that belongs to a different target than the one being installed, enabling per-target path filtering.

nodes.name: resolveBaseRoot
nodes.summary: Resolves the base filesystem root for a given installation scope ('home' resolves to the user's home directory, 'project' resolves to the current working directory).

nodes.name: listRelativeFiles
nodes.summary: Recursively lists all files under a directory, returning normalized relative paths sorted alphabetically — used to enumerate source assets for flat copy operations.

nodes.name: createManagedOperation
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a managed file-operation descriptor object with source, destination, strategy, and metadata fields — the fundamental building block for all install operation plans.

nodes.name: defaultValidateAdapterInput
nodes.summary: Validates adapter input by checking for required fields (repoRoot, installRoot) and verifying that the install root does not point to the user's home directory to prevent accidental overwrite.

nodes.name: createRemappedOperation
nodes.summary: Creates a managed operation for a source file remapped to a non-default destination path, supporting path override while preserving module metadata.

nodes.name: createNamespacedFlatRuleOperations
nodes.summary: Enumerates a source rules directory and creates managed copy operations that preserve a namespace prefix in the destination filename, handling both directory recursion and single-file cases.

nodes.name: createFlatFileOperations
nodes.summary: Enumerates a source directory and creates managed copy operations for every file, applying an optional destination name transform and flattening the output structure.

nodes.name: createInstallTargetAdapter
nodes.summary: Main factory function that assembles a frozen install-target adapter object from a config, wiring together resolveRoot, resolveDestinationPath, createScaffoldOperation, planOperations, validate, and other callbacks into a unified adapter interface.

nodes.name: defaultValidateOpencodeHome
nodes.summary: Validates that required OpenCode compiled artefacts (binaries and SSE handlers) exist at expected paths under the repo root before allowing installation to proceed.

nodes.name: isExpectedType
nodes.summary: Checks whether a filesystem path exists and matches an expected type (file or directory), returning false for missing paths rather than throwing.

nodes.name: planInstallTargetScaffold
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full installation plan for a target: resolves the adapter, validates the input (surfacing blocking errors), then delegates to the adapter's planOperations to produce the complete set of file operations.

nodes.name: classifier.py
nodes.summary: Classifies agent trace events against a compliance spec by invoking an LLM via subprocess and parsing its JSON response, mapping each event index to a matched spec step.

nodes.name: grader.py
nodes.summary: Grades a recorded agent trace against a compliance spec by orchestrating event classification, checking temporal ordering, computing per-step and overall compliance rates, and recommending hook promotion.

nodes.name: parser.py
