nodes.summary: Defines the core data model (ObservationEvent, Detector, Step, ComplianceSpec) and provides parse_trace and parse_spec functions for loading JSONL trace logs and YAML compliance specs from disk.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python dataclasses for all domain types; parse_trace expects JSONL (one JSON object per line) while parse_spec expects YAML.

nodes.name: report.py
nodes.summary: Generates a Markdown compliance report summarising overall and per-step rates, scenario outcomes, and which spec steps should be promoted to hooks based on a configurable threshold.

nodes.name: run.py
nodes.summary: Entry-point CLI for the skill-comply pipeline: parses arguments, generates a compliance spec from a skill file, generates test scenarios, runs each scenario, grades the results, and writes a Markdown report.

nodes.name: runner.py
nodes.summary: Executes each test scenario in an isolated sandbox directory by invoking Claude Code via subprocess, then parses the streaming JSON output into ObservationEvent records.

nodes.name: scenario_generator.py
nodes.summary: Generates test scenarios for a skill by prompting an LLM (via subprocess) with the skill content and compliance spec YAML, then parsing the YAML response into Scenario dataclass instances.

nodes.name: spec_generator.py
nodes.summary: Generates a ComplianceSpec YAML from a skill file by prompting an LLM via subprocess and validating the output by round-tripping it through parse_spec with retry logic.

nodes.name: utils.py
nodes.summary: Provides a single extract_yaml helper that strips optional YAML fenced-code-block delimiters from LLM output text.

nodes.name: test_grader.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the grader module covering compliant and noncompliant traces, temporal ordering of steps, hook-promotion recommendations, and edge cases such as empty traces and cross-referenced spec steps.

nodes.name: test_parser.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the parser module verifying trace parsing (event fields, ordering, empty/missing files) and spec parsing (step fields, optional detector fields, scoring threshold, required vs optional steps).

nodes.name: test_runner.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the runner module covering sandbox setup (shell builtin skipping, missing executables), max-turns termination handling, and error message formatting when scenarios fail.

nodes.name: classify_events
nodes.summary: Builds an LLM prompt from the compliance spec and trace events, invokes the classifier LLM via subprocess, and returns a mapping from event index to matched spec step ID.

nodes.name: grade
nodes.summary: Orchestrates compliance grading: classifies trace events, checks temporal ordering for each spec step, computes per-step detection and overall compliance rates, and returns a ComplianceResult.

nodes.name: StepResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding the grading outcome for a single compliance spec step: detection flag, supporting evidence events, and optional failure reason.

nodes.name: ComplianceResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing the full grading result for one compliance spec: per-step results, overall compliance rate, hook promotion recommendation, and raw classification mapping.

nodes.name: parse_trace
nodes.summary: Reads a JSONL trace file from disk and returns a timestamp-sorted list of ObservationEvent instances, raising on missing files or malformed JSON lines.

nodes.name: parse_spec
nodes.summary: Reads a YAML compliance spec file and constructs a ComplianceSpec with its Steps and Detectors, raising on missing files or missing required fields.

nodes.name: ObservationEvent
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single recorded agent event with timestamp, event type, tool name, session, and tool input/output fields.

nodes.name: ComplianceSpec
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a compliance specification with an id, name, source rule reference, version, ordered Steps, and a hook-promotion threshold.

nodes.name: generate_report
nodes.summary: Assembles a comprehensive Markdown compliance report covering the spec summary, per-scenario results with step-level detail, a cross-scenario step compliance table, and the raw event trace as a data appendix.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses command-line arguments, drives the full skill-comply pipeline (spec generation, scenario generation, scenario execution, grading, and report writing), and logs overall compliance metrics.

nodes.name: run_scenario
nodes.summary: Runs a single test scenario by provisioning a sandbox directory, invoking Claude Code via subprocess with the scenario prompt, and returning a ScenarioRun with parsed observation events.

nodes.name: _parse_stream_json
nodes.summary: Parses the streaming JSON output from a Claude Code subprocess invocation, extracting tool-use and tool-result events into ObservationEvent records while tracking incomplete block state.

nodes.name: ScenarioRun
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding the outcome of a scenario execution: the scenario definition, list of ObservationEvents captured during the run, and sandbox directory path.

nodes.name: generate_scenarios
nodes.summary: Prompts an LLM with skill content and spec YAML to generate test scenarios at varying difficulty levels, parses the YAML response, and returns a tuple of Scenario dataclasses sorted by level.

nodes.name: Scenario
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a generated test scenario with id, difficulty level, description, user prompt, and optional setup shell commands.

nodes.name: generate_spec
nodes.summary: Generates a ComplianceSpec from a skill file by prompting an LLM via subprocess, extracting the YAML response, and validating it by round-tripping through parse_spec with up to max_retries attempts.

nodes.name: extract_yaml
nodes.summary: Strips YAML fenced-code-block markers (```yaml / ```) from LLM response text, returning the bare YAML content.

nodes.name: TestGradeCompliant
nodes.summary: Test class verifying grader behaviour on a fully compliant trace: correct result type, 100% compliance rate, all required steps detected, optional steps detected, no hook promotion, and non-empty evidence.

nodes.name: TestGradeNoncompliant
nodes.summary: Test class verifying grader behaviour on a noncompliant trace: low compliance rate, temporal ordering failures, undetected steps, hook promotion recommendation, and failure reason strings.

nodes.name: TestGradeEdgeCases
nodes.summary: Test class covering grader edge cases: empty trace, compliance rate computed from required-only steps, spec_id propagation, and after_step references to later-declared spec steps.

nodes.name: TestParseTrace
nodes.summary: Test class for parse_trace covering successful parsing, timestamp ordering, event field correctness, empty-file handling, and FileNotFoundError on missing paths.

nodes.name: TestParseSpec
nodes.summary: Test class for parse_spec covering correct parsing of a TDD compliance spec, step and detector fields, optional detector field handling, scoring threshold, and required vs optional step classification.

nodes.name: TestSetupSandboxSkipsShellBuiltins
nodes.summary: Test class verifying sandbox setup skips shell builtins (cd, pushd, popd), tolerates missing executables, and still runs valid real commands.

nodes.name: TestRunScenarioMaxTurnsTermination
nodes.summary: Test class verifying that run_scenario treats a non-zero exit code as a normal termination when the max-turns marker is present in stdout, but raises RuntimeError without the marker.

nodes.name: TestRunScenarioErrorIncludesStdoutTail
nodes.summary: Test class verifying that RuntimeError messages from failed scenario runs include the tail of stdout to aid debugging.

nodes.name: github-coordination.js
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for the GitHub coordination workflow, parsing arguments and dispatching commands such as claim, sync, validate, publish, review, unblock, and decompose to the lib layer.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the CLI: parses arguments, loads policy and state store, dispatches the requested command, formats output, and handles errors with process.exit.

nodes.name: dispatchCommand
nodes.summary: Routes the parsed command name to the corresponding action function (applyClaim, applySync, applyValidate, etc.) and returns the result.

nodes.name: parseArgs
nodes.summary: Parses CLI argv into a structured options object, handling boolean flags, value flags, and positional arguments.

nodes.name: github-coordination.js
nodes.summary: Barrel module that re-exports the complete public API of the github-coordination subsystem (actions, gh-api, parsing, policy, state, store) as a single import surface.

nodes.name: actions.js
nodes.summary: Implements all coordination action handlers (claim, sync, validate, publish, review, decompose, unblock) that mutate GitHub issue state, labels, and local coordination records.

nodes.name: applyClaim
nodes.summary: Claims an epic issue for a given actor: validates inputs, builds coordination state, updates issue body and labels on GitHub, posts a comment, and persists to the local store.

nodes.name: applySync
nodes.summary: Syncs all epic issues in a repo: iterates open issues, reconciles coordination state and labels, updates issue bodies on GitHub, and upserts local store records.

nodes.name: applyValidate
nodes.summary: Validates an epic's readiness by checking dependency closure, collecting validation results, updating state and labels accordingly.

nodes.name: applyPublish
nodes.summary: Publishes a validated epic update: runs validation, builds new state, updates issue body and labels, posts a comment, and persists to the store.

nodes.name: applyReview
nodes.summary: Handles review state transitions (requested, approved, blocked) on a GitHub epic issue by updating state, labels, body, and posting a comment.

nodes.name: applyDecompose
nodes.summary: Reconciles an epic's task breakdown from its issue body, detecting open/completed tasks and updating coordination state, labels, body, and posting a comment.

nodes.name: applyUnblock
nodes.summary: Sweeps all blocked epics, checks if their dependencies are now closed, and unblocks those that are ready by updating state, labels, body, and posting comments.

nodes.name: gh-api.js
nodes.summary: Low-level GitHub CLI wrapper providing functions to normalize repos/issues/labels, run gh commands synchronously, and perform get/list/edit/comment operations on issues.

nodes.name: runCommand
nodes.summary: Executes a shell command synchronously via spawnSync and throws on non-zero exit or stderr output.

nodes.name: getIssue
nodes.summary: Fetches a single GitHub issue by repo and issue number via the gh CLI, returning a normalized issue object.

nodes.name: editIssue
nodes.summary: Updates a GitHub issue's title, body, state, and/or labels via the gh CLI, only including arguments for fields that are provided.

nodes.name: parsing.js
nodes.summary: Provides parsing and rendering utilities for GitHub issue bodies: extracts coordination state blocks, task lists, and issue references, and renders or merges coordination state back into issue bodies.

nodes.name: extractCoordinationState
nodes.summary: Extracts the embedded JSON coordination state block from a GitHub issue body using a regex-delimited marker pattern.

nodes.name: extractTasks
nodes.summary: Parses Markdown checkbox task list items from an issue body, returning structured task objects with completion status and description.

nodes.name: mergeIssueBody
nodes.summary: Merges updated coordination state into a GitHub issue body by replacing an existing state block or appending a new one.

nodes.name: policy.js
nodes.summary: Defines the default coordination policy (labels, states, branches, actors, dependencies) and loads optional project-level config overrides from a JSON file.

nodes.name: loadPolicy
nodes.summary: Loads and validates a coordination policy by merging defaults with optional project config read from a JSON file on disk.

nodes.name: state.js
nodes.summary: Manages coordination state lifecycle: initializing default state from issue data, applying action-driven state transitions, computing desired labels, syncing labels to GitHub, and building output summaries.

nodes.name: getCoordinationState
nodes.summary: Retrieves or initializes a coordination state object for an issue by parsing the embedded body block and falling back to defaults.

nodes.name: buildIssueStateFromAction
nodes.summary: Produces a new coordination state object by applying an action (claim, sync, validate, etc.) to the current state, merging in action-specific fields.

nodes.name: verifyDependenciesClosed
nodes.summary: Checks whether all dependency issue numbers for an epic are closed by fetching current issue states from GitHub.

nodes.name: store.js
nodes.summary: Bridges the github-coordination module to the persistent state-store: opens the store, maps coordination state to work-item format, and upserts coordination records.

nodes.name: upsertCoordinationWorkItem
nodes.summary: Maps a GitHub issue and its coordination state to a work-item record and upserts it into the persistent state store.

nodes.name: openStore
nodes.summary: Opens the coordination-specific SQLite state store using the configured or default home directory path.

nodes.name: github-coordination.test.js
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for the github-coordination library, covering parsing, state management, label logic, and policy loading using a custom lightweight test runner.

nodes.name: cost-tracker.js
nodes.summary: Claude Code hook that tracks API cost and token usage per session by reading harness cost files and parsing Claude session transcripts, then emitting cost-summary data for downstream consumers.

nodes.name: sumUsageFromTranscript
nodes.summary: Parses a Claude session transcript file line-by-line (JSONL), accumulating input/output/cache token counts and total cost across all usage events.

nodes.name: readHarnessCost
nodes.summary: Reads a harness-managed cost cache file from the OS temp directory for a given session, returning cost data only when within the allowed max-age window.

nodes.name: ecc-context-monitor.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse hook that monitors Claude Code session health by evaluating context window usage, cost thresholds, idle time, scope drift, and tool-call loops, emitting structured warnings to be surfaced to the user.

nodes.name: evaluateConditions
nodes.summary: Inspects the session bridge data for a suite of warning conditions (high context, cost thresholds, idle time, scope drift, tool loops), returning an array of warning objects sorted by severity.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Main hook entry point that reads stdin JSON, loads bridge data, evaluates session conditions, and writes structured output (or empty pass-through) based on active warnings and suppress state.

nodes.name: ecc-metrics-bridge.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse hook that accumulates per-session metrics (token counts, cost, recently edited files, hashed tool calls) into a shared session bridge file, enabling other hooks and the statusline to display live stats.

nodes.name: hashToolCall
nodes.summary: Produces a stable 8-character SHA-256 hash for a tool call, using content-aware fingerprinting for file-edit tools and a command-prefix + stable-JSON strategy for others, to enable loop detection.

nodes.name: readSessionCost
nodes.summary: Reads a Claude session's cost JSONL transcript file, summing token counts and cost while detecting and warning about malformed or duplicate lines via a deduplication hash.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Main hook entry point that reads stdin JSON, updates the session bridge with current tool call metrics (cost, tokens, recent files, tool hash history), then atomically persists the result.

nodes.name: ecc-statusline.js
nodes.summary: Hook that renders a terminal statusline displaying live session metrics (cost, tokens, duration, context bar, current task) by reading data from the session bridge and active agent TODO files.

nodes.name: runStatusline
nodes.summary: Main statusline renderer — reads stdin hook input, loads bridge data, optionally resets session origin, reads the current task, and writes a formatted terminal status string to stdout.

nodes.name: session-bridge.js
nodes.summary: Low-level shared-state library providing atomic read/write operations on a per-session bridge JSON file in the OS temp directory, used by all ECC hooks to share metrics between invocations.

nodes.name: writeBridgeAtomic
nodes.summary: Atomically writes a data object to the session bridge file via a temp file + rename strategy to prevent partial reads by concurrent hook processes.

nodes.name: renameWithRetry
nodes.summary: Retries a file rename on transient EBUSY/EPERM/EACCES errors with exponential backoff using Atomics.wait, tolerating the cross-process contention common on Windows.

nodes.name: readBridge
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the current session bridge JSON, returning a default empty object if the file does not yet exist.

nodes.name: sanitizeSessionId
nodes.summary: Sanitizes a raw session ID string by stripping path traversal characters and replacing unsafe characters with underscores, capping length at 128 characters.

nodes.name: ecc-context-monitor.test.js
nodes.summary: Unit and integration test suite for ecc-context-monitor.js, covering all warning conditions (context, cost thresholds, idle, scope, loops), the costWarningsEnabled flag, severity labels, and the run() entry point.

nodes.name: ecc-metrics-bridge.test.js
nodes.summary: Comprehensive test suite for ecc-metrics-bridge.js, covering tool-call hashing, file path extraction, session cost reading from JSONL transcripts (including malformed/duplicate line handling), and the run() entry point.

nodes.name: ecc-statusline.test.js
nodes.summary: Unit tests for ecc-statusline.js helper functions, covering formatDuration output at various time offsets, buildContextBar rendering at different fill ratios, and readCurrentTask file-discovery logic.

nodes.name: session-bridge.test.js
nodes.summary: Test suite for session-bridge.js covering session ID sanitization, bridge path derivation, atomic read/write roundtrips, concurrent write safety via spawned child processes, and temp-file leak prevention.

nodes.name: index.js
nodes.summary: Entry point for the ECC SQLite-backed state store: resolves the database path, wraps sql.js with a transaction-safe API, opens the database, applies migrations, and builds the full query API via createStateStore.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses sql.js (WebAssembly SQLite) instead of better-sqlite3 to stay dependency-light; wrapSqlJsDatabase adds a custom transaction/exec layer on top.

nodes.name: migrations.js
nodes.summary: Manages SQLite schema migrations for the state store: tracks applied versions in a schema_migrations table and applies pending migrations in a single transaction.

nodes.name: queries.js
nodes.summary: Defines all SQL queries and the full data-access API for the state store, covering sessions, skill runs, work items, governance events, install state, and status aggregation across ECC entities.
nodes.languageNotes: createQueryApi (lines 382–899) is a large factory function that prepares all SQL statements at construction time for reuse.

nodes.name: schema.js
nodes.summary: Loads the ECC entity JSON schema and provides AJV-based validation helpers (validateEntity, assertValidEntity) used throughout queries.js to guard all write operations.

nodes.name: sessions-cli.js
nodes.summary: CLI tool for inspecting ECC session records: lists recent sessions, shows detailed session view with workers, skill runs, and decisions — outputs as JSON or human-readable text.

nodes.name: status.js
nodes.summary: CLI tool that queries the ECC state store for a system-wide status snapshot — active sessions, skill run health, install state, governance events, work items, and GitHub coordination — and renders the result as human-readable text, Markdown, or JSON.

nodes.name: work-items.js
nodes.summary: CLI tool for managing ECC work items: lists, gets, upserts, and closes items, and syncs GitHub PRs/issues from the gh CLI into the state store as work items.

nodes.name: state-store.test.js
nodes.summary: Comprehensive integration test suite for the state store library: exercises database initialization, migrations, session/skill/work-item/governance CRUD operations, status aggregation, and CLI script invocations.

nodes.name: github-coordination.test.js
nodes.summary: Integration tests for GitHub coordination work-item sync: uses a gh CLI shim to simulate GitHub API responses and verifies that work-items.js correctly upserts PR and issue work items into the state store.

nodes.name: wrapSqlJsDatabase
nodes.summary: Wraps a raw sql.js database instance with a higher-level API (exec, pragma, all, get, run, transaction, close) that automatically persists the in-memory database to disk after each mutating operation.

nodes.name: openDatabase
nodes.summary: Opens the SQLite database file from disk (or creates a new one), loads it into sql.js memory, and sets WAL mode and foreign key pragmas.

nodes.name: createStateStore
nodes.summary: Public factory that initializes the complete state store: resolves path, opens the database, applies pending migrations, and returns the full query API bundled with a close method.

nodes.name: resolveStateStorePath
nodes.summary: Resolves the SQLite database path from ECC_STATE_STORE_PATH env var, project-local .ecc-state.db, or a default under ~/.claude/ecc/state.db.

nodes.name: applyMigrations
nodes.summary: Determines which schema migrations have not yet been applied and executes them in order inside a single database transaction, recording each applied version in schema_migrations.

nodes.name: getAppliedMigrations
nodes.summary: Queries the schema_migrations table and returns all applied migration records ordered by version.

nodes.name: createQueryApi
nodes.summary: Factory that prepares all SQL statements once at initialization time and returns a complete data-access API object covering upsert/insert/get/list operations for sessions, skill runs, work items, skill versions, decisions, install state, and governance events.

nodes.name: summarizeSkillRuns
nodes.summary: Aggregates a list of skill run records into summary statistics including success/failure counts, rate percentages, and most recent runs.

nodes.name: summarizeInstallHealth
nodes.summary: Aggregates plugin installation records into a health summary with healthy/degraded/unknown counts and per-installation detail.

nodes.name: summarizeWorkItems
nodes.summary: Aggregates work item records into a summary with open/closed/attention/total counts for status reporting.

nodes.name: getEntityValidator
nodes.summary: Returns a cached AJV validator for the named entity type, compiling from the JSON schema on first access.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point for the sessions CLI: parses args, opens the state store, dispatches to list or detail view, and prints results as JSON or human-readable output.

nodes.name: parseArgs
nodes.summary: Parses CLI arguments for the sessions tool, extracting the subcommand (list, detail), session ID, limit, and output format flags.

nodes.name: renderMarkdown
nodes.summary: Renders the full ECC status payload as a structured Markdown document with sections for skill runs, install health, work items, GitHub coordination, and governance events.

nodes.name: summarizeGithubCoordination
nodes.summary: Extracts and structures GitHub coordination summary data from work items, grouping epics, PRs, and issues with their statuses for inclusion in the status output.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point for the status CLI: opens the state store, fetches the status snapshot, optionally writes JSON and Markdown outputs to file, and prints the result.

nodes.name: parseArgs
nodes.summary: Parses CLI arguments for the status tool, extracting output format, file paths for JSON/Markdown writes, and section filter flags.

nodes.name: syncGithubWorkItems
nodes.summary: Fetches open GitHub PRs and issues via the gh CLI, upserts them into the state store as work items, and closes any stale items no longer present in GitHub.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point for the work-items CLI: dispatches to list, get, set, close, or GitHub sync subcommands against the state store.

nodes.name: buildGithubPrWorkItem
nodes.summary: Maps a GitHub PR API response object to the ECC work item format, computing status, owner, and metadata fields.

nodes.name: buildGithubIssueWorkItem
nodes.summary: Maps a GitHub issue API response object to the ECC work item format, including label extraction and metadata normalization.

nodes.name: closeStaleGithubItems
nodes.summary: Marks work items in the state store as closed if their IDs are no longer present in the set of active GitHub items for the given repo.

nodes.name: seedStore
nodes.summary: Seeds a fresh state store database with representative fixture data covering sessions, skill runs, skill versions, decisions, install state, and governance events for use in integration tests.

nodes.name: runTests
nodes.summary: Main test runner that executes the full state store test suite: verifies database initialization, migration idempotency, session listing/detail, status aggregation, work item CRUD, and CLI script output.

nodes.name: createGhShim
nodes.summary: Creates a gh CLI shim binary for testing: writes a Node.js script that serves pre-configured JSON responses based on argument patterns, enabling hermetic GitHub API simulation.

nodes.name: runTests
nodes.summary: Test runner for GitHub coordination sync scenarios: verifies that work-items.js correctly syncs PRs and issues from a gh shim into the state store and marks stale items as closed.

nodes.name: control-pane.js
nodes.summary: Entry-point script that starts the ECC control-pane HTTP server, parses CLI arguments, and opens a browser tab pointing at the local UI.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses CLI arguments, creates and starts the control-pane HTTP server, opens a browser, and registers SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers for graceful shutdown.

nodes.name: openBrowser
nodes.summary: Spawns a platform browser-open command in the background to navigate to a given URL, detaching it from the Node process.

nodes.name: actions.js
nodes.summary: Defines and builds the set of runnable control-pane actions (e.g., sync, search, run tests), constructing their CLI command-line strings from a static definition map.

nodes.name: buildControlPaneAction
nodes.summary: Looks up a named action definition, resolves working directory and arguments, and returns a fully-populated action object including its executable command-line string.

nodes.name: server.js
nodes.summary: Creates and configures the control-pane HTTP server, wiring routes for the UI, snapshot API, action execution, and static-file serving with host/origin security checks.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Node.js built-in http module with manual request routing — no framework dependency.

nodes.name: createControlPaneServer
nodes.summary: Wires all HTTP routes (UI HTML, static assets, snapshot endpoint, action endpoints) into a Node http.Server, enforcing host/origin allowlist and optional read-only mode.

nodes.name: runAction
nodes.summary: Spawns an action's shell command, captures stdout/stderr with a configurable timeout, and resolves to a structured result object including exit code and bounded output.

nodes.name: parseArgs
nodes.summary: Parses process.argv-style argument arrays into a typed config object covering host, port, database paths, query, read-only, and no-open flags.

nodes.name: state.js
nodes.summary: Reads ECC's SQLite databases to build the complete control-pane snapshot: sessions, knowledge graph entities, connector status, work items, and available actions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses sql.js (WASM SQLite) for zero-native-dependency database access; TOML config loaded via smol-toml.

nodes.name: buildControlPaneSnapshot
nodes.summary: Top-level snapshot builder that resolves config, opens databases, and aggregates sessions, knowledge entries, connector status, work items, and actions into a single payload.

nodes.name: recallKnowledgeEntries
nodes.summary: Scores and ranks knowledge graph entities against optional query terms using TF-style token matching, returning the top results with matched terms and priority metadata.

nodes.name: scoreEntity
nodes.summary: Computes a relevance score for a knowledge entity by matching query terms against weighted fields (name, type, path, summary, observations) and boosting pinned/high-priority entries.

nodes.name: resolveControlPaneConfig
nodes.summary: Discovers and deep-merges all applicable TOML config files from default search paths (home dir, cwd, parent dirs) into a normalized configuration object.

nodes.name: connectorStatus
nodes.summary: Computes the live sync status of every configured memory connector by joining config definitions with checkpoint rows from the database.

nodes.name: summarizeSessions
nodes.summary: Aggregates a list of session rows into summary statistics including total count, token usage, tool invocations, and cumulative cost.

nodes.name: summarizeWorkItems
nodes.summary: Groups work items by normalized status category (open, in-progress, done, blocked) and returns counts alongside the full item list.

nodes.name: normalizeSession
nodes.summary: Converts a raw SQLite session row into a typed session object with numeric coercion, JSON parsing for stored metadata, and unread message count injection.

nodes.name: ui.js
nodes.summary: Generates the complete control-pane single-page HTML application as a template literal, embedding all CSS, JavaScript, and React-like UI logic inline.
nodes.languageNotes: Produces a self-contained HTML page via a single large template-literal function with no build step — all UI logic runs in the browser via inline scripts.

nodes.name: renderControlPaneHtml
nodes.summary: Renders the entire control-pane single-page application as an HTML string, containing the complete client-side UI with embedded styles and JavaScript.

nodes.name: control-pane-actions.test.js
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the control-pane actions module, verifying action construction, command-line formatting, shell quoting, and error handling for invalid action IDs.

nodes.name: control-pane-state.test.js
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the control-pane state module, creating in-memory SQLite fixtures to verify snapshot building, session reading, knowledge recall, config resolution, and work-item normalization.

nodes.name: control-pane.test.js
nodes.summary: End-to-end integration tests for the control-pane entry-point script, starting a real HTTP server and verifying API responses, action execution, static file serving, and host-rebinding security.

nodes.name: post-edit-accumulator.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse hook that accumulates edited JS/TS file paths to a per-project temp file, enabling downstream stop-hooks to batch-process all files edited in a session.

nodes.name: getAccumFile
nodes.summary: Derives a per-project accumulator file path in the OS temp directory using a SHA-1 hash of the cwd.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Entry point for the hook: parses stdin JSON and appends each edited file path to the accumulator file.

nodes.name: post-edit-format.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse hook that runs a code formatter (Prettier or Biome) on each JS/TS file immediately after it is edited by Claude Code.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Reads the edited file path from stdin, detects the formatter, resolves its binary, and spawns it on the file; logs errors without blocking.

nodes.name: quality-gate.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse hook that runs lint and optional type-check on a single edited file, reporting problems to stderr without blocking Claude Code execution.

nodes.name: maybeRunQualityGate
nodes.summary: Core logic: detects formatter, resolves binary, conditionally runs format-check, ESLint, and TypeScript type-check steps based on file extension and env flags.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Entry point for the hook: parses stdin JSON and delegates to maybeRunQualityGate.

nodes.name: stop-format-typecheck.js
nodes.summary: Stop hook that reads the accumulated list of edited files, batch-formats them and runs TypeScript type-check grouped by project root, then cleans up the accumulator file.

nodes.name: formatBatch
nodes.summary: Formats a batch of files using the project's detected formatter binary, guarding against unsafe path characters before spawning.

nodes.name: typecheckBatch
nodes.summary: Runs tsc --noEmit for a tsconfig directory, filters output lines relevant to edited files, and writes them to stderr.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the stop-hook: reads accumulator, groups files by project root and tsconfig, then dispatches formatBatch and typecheckBatch in parallel.

nodes.name: resolve-formatter.js
nodes.summary: Shared library that detects the code formatter (Prettier or Biome) for a project root and resolves the formatter binary path, with in-process caching for repeated calls.

nodes.name: findProjectRoot
nodes.summary: Walks up the directory tree from startDir to find the nearest package.json, with result caching.

nodes.name: detectFormatter
nodes.summary: Inspects the project root for .prettierrc, package.json devDependencies, and .biome.json to determine which formatter is configured, caching the result.

nodes.name: resolveFormatterBin
nodes.summary: Resolves the local node_modules binary path for the detected formatter, falling back to a package-manager runner command, with caching.

nodes.name: quality-gate.test.js
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for the quality-gate hook, exercising its run() entry point with mocked stdin and verifying exit codes and stderr output.

nodes.name: stop-format-typecheck.test.js
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for the stop-format-typecheck hook, covering accumulator file management and the full format+typecheck pipeline across scenarios.

nodes.name: resolve-formatter.test.js
nodes.summary: Unit/integration test suite for the resolve-formatter library, verifying project root detection, formatter detection heuristics, and binary resolution across multiple project layouts.

nodes.name: runTests
nodes.summary: Main test orchestrator that creates temporary directories, exercises findProjectRoot, detectFormatter, resolveFormatterBin, and clearCaches across a comprehensive set of scenarios.

nodes.name: forget.mjs
nodes.summary: Implements the 'ck forget' command that removes a named context entry from the CK project registry and deletes its on-disk data.

nodes.name: info.mjs
nodes.summary: Implements the 'ck info' command that displays detailed metadata for a single named context, including its most recent session state.

nodes.name: init.mjs
nodes.summary: Implements the 'ck init' command that bootstraps a new CK context from optional CLAUDE.md and README files, extracting project metadata to seed the context store.

nodes.name: list.mjs
nodes.summary: Implements the 'ck list' command that renders a tabular summary of all registered CK contexts with staleness indicators and recent session info.

nodes.name: migrate.mjs
nodes.summary: Implements the 'ck migrate' command that converts legacy Markdown-based context files into the structured JSON context format used by the current CK system.

nodes.name: resume.mjs
nodes.summary: Implements the 'ck resume' command that looks up an existing context by name and prints a briefing box to help the agent reorient to the project state.

nodes.name: save.mjs
nodes.summary: Implements the 'ck save' command that captures the current session state — summary, decisions, next steps, blockers, and git activity — and appends it to the context store.

nodes.name: shared.mjs
nodes.summary: Core shared library for the CK (context-keeper) skill, exporting path constants, JSON/context I/O helpers, git utilities, and all rendering functions used by every CK command.
nodes.languageNotes: ESM (.mjs) module exporting 22 named symbols — functions and constants — consumed by all sibling command files; uses Node.js fs, path, os, crypto, and child_process APIs.

nodes.name: readJson
nodes.summary: Reads and parses a JSON file from disk, returning null if the file does not exist.

nodes.name: writeJson
nodes.summary: Serializes an object to JSON and writes it to disk, creating parent directories as needed.

nodes.name: readProjects
nodes.summary: Reads the CK projects registry JSON file, returning an empty object if not yet initialized.

nodes.name: writeProjects
nodes.summary: Persists the projects registry object to the CK home directory.

nodes.name: contextPath
nodes.summary: Returns the path to a context's JSON data file given its context directory.

nodes.name: contextMdPath
nodes.summary: Returns the path to a context's human-readable Markdown representation file.

nodes.name: loadContext
nodes.summary: Loads a context's JSON data object from disk given its storage directory.

nodes.name: saveContext
nodes.summary: Persists a context's JSON data and regenerates the corresponding Markdown file atomically.

nodes.name: resolveContext
nodes.summary: Resolves a user-provided context argument (name, numeric index, or fuzzy string) to a fully-loaded context object, supporting multiple match strategies.

nodes.name: today
nodes.summary: Returns today's date as an ISO YYYY-MM-DD string.

nodes.name: daysAgoLabel
nodes.summary: Converts an ISO date string to a human-readable relative time label such as 'today', 'yesterday', or 'N days ago'.

nodes.name: stalenessIcon
nodes.summary: Returns a staleness indicator character based on how many days have elapsed since the given date.

nodes.name: shortId
nodes.summary: Generates a short random hex identifier for tagging new sessions.

nodes.name: gitLogSince
nodes.summary: Returns the raw git log output for a project directory since a given date string.

nodes.name: gitSummary
nodes.summary: Aggregates git activity since a date into commit count, file change count, and a snippet of recent commit messages for display in a context summary.

nodes.name: encodeProjectPath
nodes.summary: Encodes an absolute project path into a filesystem-safe string for use as a directory name.

nodes.name: nativeMemoryDir
nodes.summary: Returns the path to the Claude Code native memory directory for a given project, mirroring the convention used by the host agent.

nodes.name: renderContextMd
nodes.summary: Renders a full Markdown document from a context object, including project metadata, all session entries, decisions, constraints, and next steps.

nodes.name: renderBriefingBox
nodes.summary: Renders a bordered terminal briefing box summarizing the most recent session state — left-off point, next steps, and blockers — for the 'ck resume' command output.

nodes.name: renderInfoBlock
nodes.summary: Renders a multi-line terminal info block showing full context detail for the 'ck info' command.

nodes.name: renderListTable
nodes.summary: Renders a padded ASCII table of all CK contexts with staleness icons, session IDs, summaries, and relative timestamps for the 'ck list' command.

nodes.name: parseLeftOff
nodes.summary: Parses the legacy 'Left Off' section from a Markdown context file, handling both bulleted and free-form session block formats.

nodes.name: parseDecisionsTable
nodes.summary: Parses a Markdown pipe-table of decisions from a legacy context file, extracting each decision row as a structured object.

nodes.name: canonical-mcp.js
nodes.summary: Core normalization and canonicalization library for MCP server entries, providing secret-redaction, transport normalization, server deduplication across harnesses, and inventory-building functions conforming to the ecc.mcp.v1 schema.
nodes.languageNotes: CommonJS module exporting 13 pure helper functions plus constants; no external dependencies, purely transforms raw MCP config objects into a canonical form.

nodes.name: looksLikeSecretValue
nodes.summary: Heuristically detects whether a string value looks like a secret (token, key, credential) using pattern matching and entropy indicators.

nodes.name: redactArgs
nodes.summary: Scans a CLI argument array and redacts values that follow secret-named flags or look like secrets, replacing them with a REDACTED placeholder.

nodes.name: normalizeTransport
nodes.summary: Maps raw transport type strings (stdio, sse, http, etc.) to their canonical ecc.mcp.v1 transport values.

nodes.name: normalizeServerEntry
nodes.summary: Converts a raw MCP server configuration object from any harness into a normalized server record conforming to the ecc.mcp.v1 schema, including secret redaction and transport normalization.

nodes.name: mergeServers
nodes.summary: Deduplicates and merges server records with the same name across multiple harnesses, tracking consistency of configuration signatures.

nodes.name: buildFragmentation
nodes.summary: Builds a fragmentation report listing servers that appear in more than one harness, sorted by name.

nodes.name: buildInventory
nodes.summary: Assembles the complete ecc.mcp.v1 inventory object from raw server records, including merged servers, harness list, fragmentation report, and secret-bearing server counts.

nodes.name: redactUrl
nodes.summary: Strips credentials from URLs and redacts query-string parameters that look like secrets.

nodes.name: summarizeEnv
nodes.summary: Summarizes an env object into a list of key names, flagging whether any key looks like a secret.

nodes.name: collect.js
nodes.summary: Orchestrates collection of MCP server records by running all registered harness readers and feeding their output through the canonical normalization pipeline.

nodes.name: collectMcpInventory
nodes.summary: Iterates over all registered reader functions, accumulates their raw server records, and returns a normalized ecc.mcp.v1 inventory.

nodes.name: claude-code.js
nodes.summary: MCP inventory reader for Claude Code, parsing the user-level and project-level settings JSON files to extract all configured MCP server entries.

nodes.name: readClaudeCodeMcp
nodes.summary: Reads Claude Code MCP server configurations from user-level and project-level settings files, returning an array of raw server records.

nodes.name: readMcpServersBlock
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the mcpServers block from a single Claude Code JSON config file, returning normalized server records tagged with harness and scope.

nodes.name: mapClaudeServer
nodes.summary: Maps a single raw Claude Code MCP server entry to a normalized raw record shape for downstream processing.

nodes.name: codex.js
nodes.summary: MCP inventory reader for OpenAI Codex, parsing the TOML-format config file at ~/.codex/config.toml to extract MCP server entries.

nodes.name: readCodexMcp
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the Codex TOML config file, extracting MCP server entries from the mcp.servers block.

nodes.name: mapCodexServer
nodes.summary: Maps a single raw Codex MCP server entry to a normalized raw record shape.

nodes.name: loadTomlParser
nodes.summary: Lazily loads a TOML parser library, using an injected implementation or falling back to a dynamic require, returning null if unavailable.

nodes.name: opencode.js
nodes.summary: MCP inventory reader for OpenCode, searching candidate JSON config paths under ~/.config/opencode to extract MCP server entries.

nodes.name: readOpencodeMcp
nodes.summary: Searches multiple candidate config file paths for an OpenCode configuration file and extracts MCP server entries from the mcp block.

nodes.name: mapOpencodeServer
nodes.summary: Maps a single raw OpenCode MCP server entry to a normalized raw record shape, handling both stdio and SSE transport types.

nodes.name: mcp-inventory.js
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for the mcp-inventory tool, which reads MCP server configurations from all installed harnesses (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode), normalizes them, and reports fragmentation (servers configured in multiple harnesses).

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Main CLI driver: parses arguments, collects the MCP inventory, and prints results in either JSON or human-readable format.

nodes.name: formatHumanReport
nodes.summary: Formats the ecc.mcp.v1 inventory as a human-readable text report showing all servers and fragmentation details.

nodes.name: parseArgs
nodes.summary: Parses process.argv flags (--json, --fragmented, --help) into a simple options object.

nodes.name: usage
nodes.summary: Returns a formatted help string describing the mcp-inventory CLI options.

nodes.name: mcp-inventory.test.js
nodes.summary: Comprehensive test suite for the mcp-inventory module, covering canonical normalization, secret redaction, reader parsing for all three harnesses (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode), and the CLI output formatter.

nodes.name: captureStdout
nodes.summary: Test helper that intercepts console.log output during a callback and returns it as a string for assertion.

nodes.name: amendify.js
nodes.summary: Generates structured skill amendment proposals by combining health report data with patch previews, producing a timestamped proposal object ready for SKILL.md updates.

nodes.name: summarizePatchPreview
nodes.summary: Formats a human-readable patch preview summary from a skill health report, listing error patterns, task patterns, and feedback patterns.

nodes.name: proposeSkillAmendment
nodes.summary: Builds a complete amendment proposal for a given skill ID by analyzing observation records and generating structured patch metadata including a preview, ID, and timestamp.

nodes.name: evaluate.js
nodes.summary: Computes a skill evaluation scaffold comparing baseline versus amended run outcomes, summarizing success rates and surfacing improvement signals.

nodes.name: buildSkillEvaluationScaffold
nodes.summary: Constructs a structured evaluation comparing baseline and amended run records for a skill, including success rates and a final improvement verdict.

nodes.name: health.js
nodes.summary: Aggregates skill observation records into a comprehensive health report per skill, tracking success rates, recurring errors, task patterns, feedback patterns, and variant runs.

nodes.name: buildSkillHealthReport
nodes.summary: Core health aggregation function that groups observation records by skill ID, computes success/failure rates, ranks recurring errors and tasks, and derives a status for each skill.

nodes.name: summarizeVariantRuns
nodes.summary: Reduces observation records into per-variant run counts and success rates for quick comparison of baseline vs. amended outcomes.

nodes.name: deriveSkillStatus
nodes.summary: Assigns a health status label (healthy, degraded, failing) to a skill based on its aggregated summary metrics.

nodes.name: observations.js
nodes.summary: Provides I/O primitives for skill telemetry observations — creating, appending, and reading NDJSON observation records from the project's telemetry directory.

nodes.name: createSkillObservation
nodes.summary: Validates and constructs a normalized skill observation record from raw input, enforcing required string fields and coercing optional fields.

nodes.name: appendSkillObservation
nodes.summary: Appends a serialized skill observation as a NDJSON line to the telemetry observations file, creating the directory if needed.

nodes.name: readSkillObservations
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the NDJSON observations file, filtering out malformed lines and returning a clean array of observation records.

nodes.name: session-inspect.js
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that dispatches session and skill inspection tasks — supporting Claude session history, dmux plan files, and the skills:health/amendify/evaluate sub-commands via the session adapter registry.

nodes.name: inspectSkillLoopTarget
nodes.summary: Dispatches skill-specific sub-commands (health, amendify, evaluate) by reading observations and invoking the appropriate skill-improvement library function.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Main async CLI driver that parses arguments, routes to skill loop or session targets, and optionally writes JSON output to a file.

nodes.name: skill-improvement.test.js
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for the skill-improvement library, covering observations I/O, health report aggregation, amendment proposal generation, and evaluation scaffold construction.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Python package entry point for the Aura integration module, re-exporting the adapter's public symbols to the package surface.

nodes.name: adapter.py
nodes.summary: Core Aura trust-reputation adapter providing HTTP-based verdict lookup for agent DIDs and a gate function that raises AuraUntrusted when an agent fails the trust check.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dependency injection for the HTTP fetch function (_fetch param) to enable deterministic testing without network calls.

nodes.name: AuraUntrusted
nodes.summary: Exception class raised by before_settle when an agent DID fails the Aura trust verdict gate.

nodes.name: AuraVerdict
nodes.summary: Data class holding the trust verdict for an agent DID, including verdict category, confidence score, history flag, and raw payload; provides factory methods from_payload and unreachable.

nodes.name: aura_verdict
nodes.summary: Fetches and returns an AuraVerdict for a given DID by calling the Aura reputation API, handling network errors as unreachable verdicts.

nodes.name: before_settle
nodes.summary: Gate function intended for use as a pre-settlement hook that raises AuraUntrusted when the agent's trust verdict does not meet the configured allow list, with fail-open/fail-closed behavior for unreachable endpoints.

nodes.name: fixtures.py
nodes.summary: Test fixture factories for the Aura adapter tests, providing make_fetch (a DID-keyed stub fetch function) and raising_fetch (a fetch stub that raises a configurable exception).

nodes.name: make_fetch
nodes.summary: Factory returning a stub HTTP fetch function keyed on DID query parameter, used to drive aura_verdict tests with controlled responses.

nodes.name: raising_fetch
nodes.summary: Factory returning a fetch stub that unconditionally raises a given exception, used to simulate unreachable Aura endpoints in tests.

nodes.name: test_adapter.py
nodes.summary: Pytest test suite for the Aura adapter covering verdict class contracts, gate allow/reject logic across all trust categories, fail-open/fail-closed unreachable behavior, and DID validation.

nodes.name: discussion-audit.js
nodes.summary: CLI script that audits GitHub discussions across one or more repos, checking for maintainer-touch gaps and missing accepted answers, then renders the results as text, JSON, or Markdown.

nodes.name: github-discussions.js
nodes.summary: Library module providing GitHub Discussions data access via the gh CLI, including functions to fetch discussion summaries, evaluate maintainer-touch and accepted-answer status, and summarize individual discussions.

nodes.name: operator-readiness-dashboard.js
nodes.summary: Large CLI script that generates an ECC operator readiness dashboard by evaluating dozens of artifact-level requirements (roadmap tracking, supply chain, linear progress, agent-shield, legacy salvage) against local repo files and live GitHub data, then renders a structured report.
nodes.languageNotes: At 1057 non-empty lines with 40 functions, this is the most complex single script in the batch; buildRequirements alone spans 345 lines and calls 20+ helper functions.

nodes.name: platform-audit.js
nodes.summary: CLI script and library providing platform-level readiness checks including git working-tree inspection, GitHub PR/issue queue fetching, local evidence checks, and structured report rendering in text, JSON, or Markdown.

nodes.name: parseArgs
nodes.summary: Parses argv to produce a validated options object including format, write path, repos list, first-n, and flag settings for the discussion audit CLI.

nodes.name: buildReport
nodes.summary: Fetches discussion summaries for all configured repos and aggregates them into a structured audit report object with per-repo breakdowns, totals, and pass/fail checks.

nodes.name: renderText
nodes.summary: Serializes an audit report to a human-readable plain-text format with per-check status lines and summary counts.

nodes.name: renderMarkdown
nodes.summary: Serializes an audit report to a Markdown table format, escaping pipe characters in cell values.

nodes.name: renderReport
nodes.summary: Dispatches rendering to the appropriate format handler (JSON, Markdown, or text) based on the format option.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Top-level async entry point: parses args, builds the discussion audit report, renders output, optionally writes to file, and exits with code 2 when checks fail and --exit-code is set.

nodes.name: runCommand
nodes.summary: Executes a shell command synchronously via spawnSync, throws on non-zero exit or stderr output, and returns trimmed stdout.

nodes.name: runGhJson
nodes.summary: Invokes the gh CLI with the provided args and parses the JSON response, throwing a descriptive error on parse failure.

nodes.name: discussionNeedsMaintainerTouch
nodes.summary: Returns true when a discussion was not opened by a maintainer and has no maintainer comment, using an allowlist of MAINTAINER_ASSOCIATIONS.

nodes.name: fetchDiscussionSummary
nodes.summary: Fetches a paginated list of GitHub discussions for a repo via GraphQL through gh, filters them for maintainer-touch and accepted-answer gaps, and returns a structured summary object.

nodes.name: parseArgs
nodes.summary: Parses argv for the operator-readiness-dashboard CLI, supporting format, write path, root dir, repos, budget flags, allow-untracked paths, and generated-at override.

nodes.name: buildRequirements
nodes.summary: Evaluates all operator-readiness requirements by reading local repo artifacts and the platform report, producing a flat array of requirement objects with status, evidence, and gap fields.

nodes.name: buildReport
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full dashboard report build: resolves paths, collects platform report, evaluates requirements, determines readiness, and assembles the final report object.

nodes.name: eccToolsNextLevelEvidence
nodes.summary: Scans the roadmap text for evidence of ECC next-level tools across multiple feature groups (marketplace, agents, skills, commands, hooks), returning a summary string.

nodes.name: eccToolsNextLevelGap
nodes.summary: Identifies gaps in ECC next-level tools coverage by checking which feature groups are missing from the roadmap and returns a descriptive gap string.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point for the operator-readiness-dashboard CLI: parses args, builds and renders the report, optionally writes output, and exits with code 2 on unreadiness when --exit-code is set.

nodes.name: parseArgs
nodes.summary: Parses argv for the platform-audit CLI supporting format, write path, root dir, repos, PR/issue/dirty-file budgets, allow-untracked paths, and skip-github flag.

nodes.name: inspectGit
nodes.summary: Runs git status on the repo root and returns parsed branch name, dirty file lines, and counts of blocking untracked/modified files.

nodes.name: fetchGithubRepo
nodes.summary: Fetches open PRs, open issues, and discussion summaries for a single GitHub repo using the gh CLI, returning a per-repo data object.

nodes.name: buildGithubReport
nodes.summary: Collects GitHub queue and discussion data for all configured repos, aggregates per-repo reports, and returns a combined GitHub status object.

nodes.name: buildLocalEvidenceChecks
nodes.summary: Reads local repo artifact files and returns an array of check objects evaluating the presence and content of CHANGELOG, README, CONTRIBUTING, SECURITY, and other required files.

nodes.name: buildReport
nodes.summary: Assembles the full platform audit report by combining git inspection, GitHub repo data, and local evidence checks into a unified check list with overall pass/fail status.

nodes.name: renderMarkdown
nodes.summary: Renders the platform audit report as a Markdown document with a summary header, a status table with emoji indicators per check, and a failures section.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Top-level async entry point for platform-audit CLI: parses args, builds report, renders output, optionally writes to file, and exits with code 2 on failure when --exit-code is set.

nodes.name: git.js
nodes.summary: Low-level git abstraction layer for worktree lifecycle analysis, providing a factory that creates a hermetic git runner with operations such as listing worktrees, detecting dirty state, computing ahead/behind counts, and predicting merge conflicts via git merge-tree.

nodes.name: hermeticGitEnv
nodes.summary: Builds a sanitized environment object for git subprocess invocations, stripping variables that could affect git behavior and ensuring reproducible, hermetic execution.

nodes.name: defaultRunImpl
nodes.summary: Default implementation for spawning a synchronous git subprocess, returning status, stdout, and stderr with the hermetic environment applied.

nodes.name: createGitRunner
nodes.summary: Factory function that creates a git runner object scoped to a repository root, exposing methods for worktree enumeration, dirty-state detection, ahead/behind computation, last-commit timestamps, and non-destructive merge-conflict prediction.

nodes.name: lifecycle.js
nodes.summary: Worktree lifecycle analysis module that classifies each worktree into states (dirty, merge-ready, conflict, merged, stale, idle), builds a comprehensive lifecycle report for a repository, and plans safe cleanup by categorizing worktrees into remove, salvage, and keep buckets.

nodes.name: classifyWorktree
nodes.summary: Classifies a worktree into a lifecycle state (dirty, conflict, merge-ready, merged, stale, or idle) based on computed facts such as dirty flag, conflict files, ahead/behind counts, and age.

nodes.name: analyzeWorktree
nodes.summary: Gathers git facts for a single worktree (dirty state, ahead/behind, last-commit timestamp, merge conflicts), then calls classifyWorktree to determine its lifecycle state and returns an enriched analysis object.

nodes.name: buildLifecycleReport
nodes.summary: Enumerates all worktrees in a repository, runs analyzeWorktree on each, and aggregates results into a structured lifecycle report with summary counts for dirty, conflict, and stale states.

nodes.name: planCleanup
nodes.summary: Produces a safe cleanup plan from a lifecycle report, partitioning worktrees into remove (safe to delete), salvage (needs committing/pushing), and keep (main, dirty, or active) lists with justification.

nodes.name: worktree-lifecycle.js
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for the worktree-lifecycle tool that parses command-line arguments, invokes the lifecycle report builder, and formats the output as human-readable text or JSON with support for filtering by conflict or stale state and showing a cleanup plan.

nodes.name: parseArgs
nodes.summary: Parses process.argv for flags (--json, --conflicts, --stale, --cleanup-plan, --base, --stale-days, --repo, --help) into a structured options object, applying defaults for base branch and stale threshold.

nodes.name: formatReport
nodes.summary: Formats a lifecycle report into human-readable output, rendering conflict files, stale worktrees with age in days, cleanup plan summaries, or a full worktree summary table depending on the active filter flags.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the CLI flow: parses arguments, optionally shows usage help, builds the lifecycle report, then outputs JSON or formatted text depending on flags.

nodes.name: worktree-lifecycle.test.js
nodes.summary: Comprehensive test suite for the worktree-lifecycle module, covering worktree classification logic, lifecycle report building, cleanup plan generation, and CLI output formatting using a lightweight test harness with a fake git runner for isolation.

nodes.name: fakeGit
nodes.summary: Test helper that creates a git runner backed by a lookup table of canned command responses instead of spawning real git processes, enabling deterministic unit testing of lifecycle logic.

nodes.name: fullRepoGit
nodes.summary: Constructs a fakeGit instance representing a full multi-worktree repo scenario with six worktrees (main, ready, conflict, merged, stale, dirty) for comprehensive integration-style tests.

nodes.name: resolve-ecc-root.js
nodes.summary: Resolves the ECC plugin root directory at runtime, trying CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT env var, standard ~/.claude install, legacy plugin paths, and plugin cache auto-detection in order; also exports a minified inline resolver string for embedding in command Markdown scripts.

nodes.name: resolveEccRoot
nodes.summary: Attempts five ordered strategies to locate the ECC source root: env var override, standard ~/.claude install, exact legacy plugin paths, plugin cache directory scan, and final fallback to ~/.claude.

nodes.name: command-plugin-root.test.js
nodes.summary: Tests that ECC command Markdown files (sessions.md, skill-health.md, instinct-status.md) embed the shared INLINE_RESOLVE resolver snippet correctly and do not contain stale hard-coded legacy paths.

nodes.name: resolve-ecc-root.test.js
nodes.summary: Comprehensive integration test suite for resolveEccRoot, covering standard install, legacy plugin roots, plugin cache auto-detection, env var override, and fallback behavior via temp-directory filesystem fixtures and child-process inline resolver execution.

nodes.name: runTests
nodes.summary: Main test runner that exercises all resolveEccRoot code paths using temp directories for isolation, covering env var, standard install, legacy slugs, plugin cache, and inline resolver invocations.

nodes.name: setupStandardInstall
nodes.summary: Creates a standard ECC installation fixture in a temporary home directory by writing a probe file at the expected scripts/lib/utils.js path.

nodes.name: setupLegacyPluginInstall
nodes.summary: Creates a legacy plugin install fixture under ~/.claude/plugins/<segments> in a temporary home directory for testing backward-compatibility path resolution.

nodes.name: setupPluginCache
nodes.summary: Creates a plugin cache fixture under ~/.claude/plugins/cache/<slug>/<org>/<version> in a temporary home directory for testing cache auto-detection.

nodes.name: tmux-worktree-orchestrator.js
nodes.summary: Core library implementing the tmux-based git worktree orchestration engine — builds orchestration plans, provisions worktree branches, seeds files, launches tmux sessions for parallel agent workers, and handles rollback on failure.
nodes.languageNotes: CommonJS module with injectable dependency seams (Impl-suffix parameters in executePlan) enabling fine-grained unit testing without spawning real processes.

nodes.name: orchestrate-worktrees.js
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for the worktree orchestrator — parses arguments, loads a JSON plan config, and delegates to the library to dry-run, write-only materialize, or fully execute the orchestration plan via tmux.

nodes.name: tmux-worktree-orchestrator.test.js
nodes.summary: Unit test suite for the tmux worktree orchestrator library, covering plan building, seed-path normalization, execution sequencing, and rollback behavior using mock implementations of fs, spawnSync, and child process calls.

nodes.name: buildOrchestrationPlan
nodes.summary: Transforms a raw plan config (session name, worker list, repo root) into a fully resolved orchestration plan with per-worker branch names, worktree paths, coordination file paths, tmux commands, and launcher command strings.

nodes.name: executePlan
nodes.summary: Executes an orchestration plan end-to-end: creates a tmux session, provisions git worktrees and branches, materializes coordination files, seeds file overlays, launches worker panes, and rolls back on any error.

nodes.name: rollbackCreatedResources
nodes.summary: Rolls back all successfully provisioned resources (tmux session, worktrees, branches, coordination directories) when plan execution fails, collecting and reporting any rollback errors.

nodes.name: cleanupExisting
nodes.summary: Tears down any existing tmux session, git worktrees, branches, and coordination directories that match the plan before a fresh execution run.

nodes.name: buildWorkerArtifacts
nodes.summary: Generates the task file content, handoff template, and status file content for a single worker from its plan definition.

nodes.name: normalizeSeedPaths
nodes.summary: Validates and normalizes a list of seed file paths, ensuring they are relative, contained within the repo root, and deduplicated.

nodes.name: overlaySeedPaths
nodes.summary: Copies seed files from the main repo into a worktree directory, creating parent directories as needed and replacing any existing files.

nodes.name: renderTemplate
nodes.summary: Resolves {variable} placeholders in a launcher command template string against a variables map, throwing on unknown keys.

nodes.name: runCommand
nodes.summary: Executes a shell command synchronously via spawnSync and throws a descriptive error if the command fails.

nodes.name: listWorktrees
nodes.summary: Returns a list of canonicalized worktree paths currently registered in the given git repository by parsing `git worktree list --porcelain` output.

nodes.name: canonicalizePath
nodes.summary: Resolves symlinks on an existing filesystem path; falls back to resolving the real parent directory joined with the basename for paths that do not yet exist.

nodes.name: materializePlan
nodes.summary: Writes all worker coordination files (task, handoff, status) for a plan to disk without executing any git or tmux commands.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI main function that orchestrates argument parsing, plan loading, and dispatches to dry-run, write-only, or full execution modes.

nodes.name: printDryRun
nodes.summary: Formats and prints a JSON dry-run preview of the orchestration plan showing workers, branches, worktree paths, and resolved git and tmux commands.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: Primary CI pipeline running tests across a 3×3×4 OS/Node/package-manager matrix, component validation (agents, hooks, commands, skills, install manifests, workflow security, rules, catalog), security audit, code coverage, and ESLint/markdownlint linting on every push to main or release branches and on all pull requests.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses concurrency groups to cancel duplicate runs and pins all action SHAs for supply-chain hardness.

nodes.name: generator-generic-ossf-slsa3-publish.yml
nodes.summary: Generates SLSA level-3 provenance for the npm package artifact on every published GitHub Release, building the package, hashing it, and delegating to the slsa-framework reusable generator workflow to produce a signed attestation.
nodes.languageNotes: Delegates provenance signing to the upstream slsa-framework/slsa-github-generator reusable workflow at a pinned SHA.

nodes.name: maintenance.yml
nodes.summary: Weekly scheduled maintenance pipeline that checks for outdated npm packages, runs a security audit, and marks stale issues and pull requests for closure using the actions/stale action.

nodes.name: monthly-metrics.yml
nodes.summary: Monthly scheduled pipeline that collects npm download counts, GitHub repo stats (stars, forks, contributors, releases), and traffic data for ecc-universal and ecc-agentshield, then upserts a running metrics table into a pinned GitHub issue for sponsor and partner reporting.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses actions/github-script with an inline JavaScript payload (~180 lines) that calls both the GitHub REST API and npmjs.org download API.

nodes.name: release-announce.yml
nodes.summary: Triggers on GitHub Release publication and posts an announcement to a Discord channel and GitHub Discussions by running the scripts/discord/release-announce.mjs script with release metadata injected as environment variables.

nodes.name: release.yml
nodes.summary: Tag-triggered release pipeline that verifies the version tag format, checks package.json alignment, runs an IOC supply-chain scan, packs the npm artifact, creates a GitHub Release with generated highlights, and publishes to npm with provenance attestation.
nodes.languageNotes: Splits work into a verify job and a publish job to keep npm credentials narrowly scoped to the final publish step.

nodes.name: reusable-release.yml
nodes.summary: Reusable workflow (workflow_call + workflow_dispatch) that encapsulates the full verify-then-publish release sequence for a given tag, enabling callers to trigger or re-trigger a release without pushing a new tag.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirrors release.yml logic but takes the tag as a workflow input, making it independently invocable from the GitHub UI or other workflows.

nodes.name: reusable-test.yml
nodes.summary: Reusable workflow (workflow_call) that runs the full test suite for a configurable OS, Node.js version, and package manager, mirroring the ci.yml test job for composition by other workflows.
nodes.languageNotes: Parameterized via workflow_call inputs so callers control the OS, Node version, and package manager without duplicating setup logic.

nodes.name: reusable-validate.yml
nodes.summary: Reusable workflow (workflow_call) that runs the full ECC component validation suite — agents, hooks, commands, skills, install manifests, workflow security, rules, unicode safety, and no-personal-paths checks — against a configurable Node.js version.
nodes.languageNotes: Intended to be called from ci.yml or other orchestrators, centralising all component-level validate-*.js script invocations.

nodes.name: supply-chain-watch.yml
nodes.summary: Runs every 6 hours to verify npm registry signatures, run IOC (indicators of compromise) scans against fixture snapshots, refresh advisory source data, validate workflow hardening rules, and upload a JSON report artifact for audit trail purposes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a non-cancel-in-progress concurrency setting so no scheduled watch run is silently skipped.

nodes.name: PLUGIN_SCHEMA_NOTES.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive reference capturing undocumented but enforced constraints of the Claude Code plugin manifest validator, including forbidden fields (agents, hooks), required fields (version), array-only shapes, and the mcpServers opt-out — grounded in real installation failures and a flip-flop history of regressions.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Short orientation document for the .claude-plugin directory that summarizes the most critical plugin manifest gotchas and explains how ECC behaves with custom LLM endpoints and gateways.

nodes.name: marketplace.json
nodes.summary: Marketplace registration manifest for the ECC plugin, declaring the plugin name, owner, description, and a single plugin entry pointing at the repository root for discovery and installation via the Claude Code marketplace.

nodes.name: plugin.json
nodes.summary: Primary Claude Code plugin manifest for ECC v2.0.0, declaring the plugin identity, skills and commands directory paths, an explicit empty mcpServers opt-out to prevent auto-discovery of root MCP definitions, and the full keyword/license/repository metadata.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an explicit `mcpServers: {}` opt-out to prevent Claude Code from auto-bundling root .mcp.json MCP servers into plugin installs; `agents` and `hooks` fields are intentionally absent because Claude Code v2.1+ auto-discovers them by convention.

nodes.name: .coderabbit.yaml
nodes.summary: CodeRabbit AI code-review bot configuration enabling early access features, setting tone instructions, and configuring automated PR review behavior for the ECC repository.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template environment file documenting all required and optional environment variables including API keys, GitHub tokens, and runtime configuration flags for the ECC harness.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents 9 env vars including ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN, and feature flags like ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING.

nodes.name: .markdownlint.json
nodes.summary: Markdownlint configuration tuning 14 linting rules for ECC's extensive Markdown documentation, selectively disabling or relaxing rules such as line length (MD013) and inline HTML (MD033).

nodes.name: .mcp.json
nodes.summary: Project-level MCP (Model Context Protocol) server configuration defining available MCP servers for use within ECC's Claude Code environment.

nodes.name: .yarnrc.yml
nodes.summary: Minimal Yarn configuration setting the node-modules linker strategy for compatibility with the Node.js project.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive agent instruction file for ECC covering available agents, orchestration patterns, security guidelines, coding style, testing requirements, and architecture patterns that Claude Code agents must follow when working in this repository.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Version history from v1.8.0 through v2.0.0 documenting added agents, skills, commands, hooks, bug fixes, and infrastructure changes across ECC releases using Keep-a-Changelog format.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Primary Claude Code instruction file for the ECC repository, providing project overview, prompt defense baseline, architecture map, key slash commands, development notes, contributing format, and skill-to-file mapping for in-repo work.

nodes.name: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
nodes.summary: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct v2.1 defining community standards, enforcement responsibilities, scope, and a four-tier enforcement guideline for the ECC project.

nodes.name: COMMANDS-QUICK-REF.md
nodes.summary: Quick-reference cheat sheet cataloguing all ECC slash commands grouped by category (workflow, testing, review, planning, session management, etc.) with brief descriptions and a decision guide for selecting the right command.

nodes.name: CONTRIBUTING.md
nodes.summary: Detailed contributor guide covering how to add skills, agents, hooks, commands, and translations to ECC, including templates, checklists, PR process, cross-harness support (Cursor, Codex, OpenCode), and naming conventions.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project README — a comprehensive 1849-line reference covering ECC's purpose as an agent harness OS, installation paths (plugin and manual), component catalog, key concepts, multi-IDE support (Cursor, Codex, Zed, OpenCode, GitHub Copilot), FAQ, token optimization guidance, and sponsorship.

nodes.name: README.zh-CN.md
nodes.summary: Simplified Chinese translation of the ECC README providing the same installation, concepts, component catalog, and ecosystem tools overview for Chinese-speaking users.
nodes.languageNotes: Simplified Chinese (zh-CN) translation; maintains structural parity with the English README across installation paths, hook runtime controls, and key concepts.

nodes.name: RULES.md
nodes.summary: Concise rule summary defining agent must-always and must-never behaviors, plus canonical format specifications for agents, skills, hooks, and commit messages.

nodes.name: SECURITY.md
nodes.summary: Security policy detailing supported versions, vulnerability reporting procedures, in-scope attack surfaces, supply-chain rules, operational guidance for secrets and MCP ports, and triage procedures for suspicious system-reminder blocks.

nodes.name: SOUL.md
nodes.summary: Short philosophical identity document stating ECC's core identity, guiding principles, agent orchestration philosophy, and cross-harness vision in 17 lines.

nodes.name: SPONSORING.md
nodes.summary: Sponsorship information for ECC explaining why sponsors matter, sponsorship tier benefits, reporting commitments, and scope expectations.

nodes.name: SPONSORS.md
nodes.summary: Active sponsor listing organized by tier (Strategic, Business, Team, Pro, Builder, Supporters) with tier descriptions and a grandfather clause for existing sponsors.

nodes.name: TROUBLESHOOTING.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive troubleshooting guide with 25 sections covering memory and context issues, agent harness failures, hook errors, installation problems, performance issues, and common error messages with step-by-step resolutions.

nodes.name: WORKING-CONTEXT.md
nodes.summary: Living project management document tracking current state, active PR queues, open constraints, interface descriptions, and an update rule so agents maintain accurate working context across sessions.

nodes.name: agent.yaml
nodes.summary: Top-level ECC plugin manifest defining the agent identity (name, version, model preferences), and enumerating the full catalog of skills and commands published by the ECC package.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a custom 'spec_version' field — ECC's gitagent export format. Enumerates 140+ skills and 90+ commands as plain string lists.

nodes.name: greptile.json
nodes.summary: Greptile AI code-review bot configuration controlling review strictness, PR trigger conditions, output sections, and per-scope security rules that flag prompt injection and supply-chain risks specific to ECC's agentic codebase.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a `customContext.rules` array to attach scope-targeted review instructions — effectively embedding security policy into the review bot config.

nodes.name: install.ps1
nodes.summary: Windows PowerShell entrypoint for the ECC installer that resolves symlinks to find the real package root, auto-installs npm dependencies if missing, then delegates to the Node.js `scripts/install-apply.js` runtime.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Set-StrictMode and $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop' for robust error handling; handles array-typed symlink targets as a PowerShell quirk.

nodes.name: install.sh
nodes.summary: POSIX shell entrypoint for the ECC installer that resolves symlinked npm bin paths to the real package root, auto-installs npm dependencies if missing, and execs the Node.js `scripts/install-apply.js` runtime with MSYS2/Git Bash path conversion support.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses cygpath for MSYS2/Git Bash compatibility so Windows Node.js receives a valid Win32 path rather than a doubled POSIX path.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root npm package manifest for `ecc-universal` v2.0.0, declaring the full file inclusion list for multi-harness distribution (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, OpenCode), npm bin entries, build/test/lint scripts, dependencies, and Node.js >=18 engine requirement.
nodes.languageNotes: The `files` array spans many harness-specific dotdirectories (.claude-plugin, .codex, .cursor, .gemini, .opencode, etc.), making the distribution multi-target by design.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python project configuration for the `llm-abstraction` package — a provider-agnostic LLM abstraction layer supporting Anthropic, OpenAI, and Ollama — with hatchling build backend, ruff/mypy linting, and pytest/coverage tooling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses hatchling as build backend with packages rooted at `src/llm`; the `llm-select` CLI entry point maps to `llm.cli.selector:main`.

nodes.name: the-longform-guide.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive deep-dive guide to Claude Code covering context and memory management, continuous learning, token optimization, verification loops, parallelization patterns, groundwork setup, sub-agent best practices, and productivity tips including voice transcription and terminal aliases.
nodes.languageNotes: 18 sections covering both conceptual ECC usage patterns and concrete operational techniques; companion to the-shortform-guide.md.
