nodes.name: the-security-guide.md

nodes.name: the-shortform-guide.md
nodes.summary: Concise reference guide to Claude Code fundamentals covering skills, commands, hooks, subagents, rules/memory, MCPs, plugins, tips (parallel workflows, keyboard shortcuts, sandboxing), and the author's personal ECC setup including plugins, MCP servers, hooks, and rules structure.
nodes.languageNotes: 27 sections; functions as an opinionated quick-reference companion to the-longform-guide.md, including personal setup details and editor preferences (Zed vs VSCode/Cursor).

nodes.summary: Defines the /orch-add-feature command that delegates a new feature addition task to an orchestrator agent, specifying usage and expected behavior.

nodes.name: orch-build-mvp.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /orch-build-mvp command that delegates building an MVP to an orchestrator agent, scoping the task to a minimal viable product.

nodes.name: orch-change-feature.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /orch-change-feature command that delegates a feature modification task to an orchestrator agent.

nodes.name: orch-fix-defect.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /orch-fix-defect command that delegates defect investigation and fixing to an orchestrator agent.

nodes.name: orch-refine-code.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /orch-refine-code command that delegates code quality refinement and cleanup to an orchestrator agent.

nodes.name: plan-prd.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /plan-prd command implementing a 4-phase PRD generation workflow (Frame, Ground, Decide, Generate), producing a structured Product Requirements Document with template and handoff instructions.

nodes.name: plan.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /plan command that converts an idea or PRD into a structured implementation plan grounded in learned patterns, with optional planner agent delegation and PRD artifact output.

nodes.name: pm2.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /pm2 command that initializes PM2 process manager configuration for a multi-service project, auto-detecting services and generating ecosystem.config.cjs, wrapper scripts, and per-service command files with Windows-specific PowerShell support.

nodes.name: pr.md
nodes.summary: Six-phase slash command specification for creating pull requests: validates branch state, discovers PR template and commit history, pushes to remote, creates the PR via gh CLI, verifies CI checks, and outputs a summary with the PR URL.

nodes.name: project-init.md
nodes.summary: Specification for the /project-init command that auto-detects project stack and generates a CLAUDE.md configuration file, with safety rules to avoid overwriting existing files and a planning flow covering detection inputs, output contract, and related commands.

nodes.name: projects.md
nodes.summary: Concise specification for the /projects command that lists all known ECC projects with their session/plan metadata, describing the implementation and usage patterns.

nodes.name: promote.md
nodes.summary: Specification for the /promote command that publishes a locally built ECC plugin snapshot to the live install, covering the implementation steps and usage instructions.

nodes.name: prp-commit.md
nodes.summary: Four-phase smart commit command specification that assesses staged changes, interprets intent, constructs conventional commit messages with semantic staging, and outputs the result; part of the PRP (Pull Request Pipeline) workflow suite.

nodes.name: prp-implement.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive six-phase implementation command specification covering environment detection, plan loading, branch preparation, task execution, multi-level validation (static analysis, unit tests, build, integration, edge cases), and implementation reporting; the execution engine for the PRP pipeline.

nodes.name: prp-plan.md
nodes.summary: Highly detailed seven-phase planning command specification (detect, parse, explore, research, design, architect, generate) that transforms a feature description or PRD into a structured implementation plan with codebase analysis across 8 categories and 5 traces; the planning engine for the PRP pipeline.

nodes.name: prp-pr.md
nodes.summary: Six-phase pull request creation command specification optimized for the PRP pipeline, similar to pr.md but with PRP-artifact-aware discovery that reads PRP plans and PRDs to enrich the PR description.

nodes.name: prp-prd.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive eight-phase Product Requirements Document (PRD) generator command specification that conducts structured discovery interviews covering problem definition, market context, technical feasibility, and scope decisions before writing a complete PRD following an embedded template.

nodes.name: prune.md
nodes.summary: Minimal specification for the /prune command that removes stale or superseded pending-instincts entries from the ECC knowledge base, describing the implementation and usage.

nodes.name: python-review.md
nodes.summary: Detailed Python code review command specification with tiered finding categories (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM), automated linting/type-check commands, framework-specific guidance for Django/FastAPI/Flask, and concrete common-fix examples with before/after code snippets.

nodes.name: quality-gate.md
nodes.summary: Specification for the /quality-gate command that runs the full project quality-gate suite (lint, type-check, tests, build) and reports pass/fail status, accepting a target-path argument to scope the run.

nodes.name: react-build.md
nodes.summary: React build-and-fix iterative loop command specification that diagnoses TypeScript, ESLint, and bundler errors, applies fixes in a countdown loop, and stops when the build is clean or a stop condition is hit.

nodes.name: react-review.md
nodes.summary: React-specific code review command specification with tiered finding categories, automated ESLint/TypeScript checks, React-specific review guidance (hooks rules, rendering, accessibility, state management), and approval criteria.

nodes.name: react-test.md
nodes.summary: React TDD command specification implementing the red-green-refactor cycle with runner auto-detection (Jest/Vitest), detailed test pattern guidance (behavior over implementation, MSW for network, accessibility, custom hooks), and anti-patterns to avoid.

nodes.name: refactor-clean.md
nodes.summary: Six-step dead-code cleanup and refactoring command specification that detects unused exports/imports/files, categorizes them as SAFE/CAUTION/SKIP, applies safe deletions, consolidates duplicates, and produces a cleanup summary.

nodes.name: resume-session.md
nodes.summary: Four-step session resumption command specification that locates and reads a saved session file, reconstructs full project context, and presents a structured status summary before awaiting the user's next instruction.

nodes.name: review-pr.md
nodes.summary: Concise pull request review command specification that uses the gh CLI to fetch PR diff and metadata, applies confidence-gated inline commenting rules, and skips low-confidence findings.

nodes.name: rust-build.md
nodes.summary: Rust build-and-fix iterative loop command specification that runs cargo check/clippy/build in sequence, applies borrow-checker and lifetime fixes per error, and tracks a countdown until the build is clean.

nodes.name: rust-review.md
nodes.summary: Rust-specific code review command specification with tiered finding categories emphasizing memory safety, ownership, and idiomatic Rust patterns, plus cargo-based automated checks and approval criteria.

nodes.name: rust-test.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /rust-test slash command that drives a full Rust TDD workflow, covering the red-green-refactor cycle, unit/parameterized/async/property-based test patterns, coverage commands, and best practices for cargo test.

nodes.name: santa-loop.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /santa-loop command that orchestrates a dual-reviewer code-quality gate (Claude agent + optional external model), running a NAUGHTY/NICE verdict loop that blocks pushes until all review criteria are satisfied.

nodes.name: save-session.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /save-session command that persists the current Claude Code session context to a structured Markdown file under a sessions/ folder, capturing goals, progress, key decisions, next steps, and open questions for later resumption.

nodes.name: security-scan.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /security-scan command that runs a deterministic security audit combining static-analysis tools (semgrep, bandit, npm audit) with an LLM review checklist, producing a structured SARIF-compatible output and a CI integration pattern.

nodes.name: sessions.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /sessions command with sub-actions (list, load, alias, remove-alias, info, list-aliases) for managing persisted session files, enabling named references and seamless context restoration across Claude Code sessions.

nodes.name: setup-pm.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /setup-pm command that detects and configures the correct package manager (npm, pnpm, yarn, bun) for the current project via a priority-ordered detection strategy, storing the result in global or project-level configuration.

nodes.name: skill-create.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /skill-create command that analyzes a project's git history and file patterns to auto-generate a SKILL.md workflow file and optional instincts, enabling teams to capture and share project-specific development conventions.

nodes.name: skill-health.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /skill-health command that renders a dashboard showing skill coverage, gaps, last-used timestamps, and actionable remediation advice across the installed skill library.

nodes.name: test-coverage.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /test-coverage command that detects the project's test framework, analyzes existing coverage reports, generates missing tests for uncovered paths, and produces a coverage summary with focus-area recommendations.

nodes.name: update-codemaps.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /update-codemaps command that scans the project structure, generates structured codemap files capturing directory layouts and key file purposes, diffs against previous codemaps to surface changes, and attaches metadata for freshness tracking.

nodes.name: update-docs.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /update-docs command that refreshes project documentation (script reference, environment docs, contributing guide, runbook) from source-of-truth files and flags stale sections for review.

nodes.name: vue-review.md
nodes.summary: Defines the /vue-review command that performs a Vue.js-specific code review covering Composition API patterns, reactivity correctness, component architecture, performance, and accessibility, with severity-tiered findings (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM).

nodes.name: dev.md
nodes.summary: Defines the Development behavioral context for the ECC harness, prescribing an implementation-first mode with priorities (working → right → clean), preferred tools (Edit, Write, Bash, Grep), and guidance to write code before explaining.

nodes.name: research.md
nodes.summary: Defines the Research behavioral context for the ECC harness, prescribing an exploration-first mode with a structured five-step research process (understand → explore → hypothesize → verify → summarize) and preferred tools including WebSearch and Task agents.

nodes.name: review.md
nodes.summary: Defines the Code Review behavioral context for the ECC harness, prescribing a quality-focused mode with a severity-ordered checklist (logic, edge cases, error handling, security, performance, readability, test coverage) and grouped output format.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Rust package manifest for the ecc-tui binary, defining the ECC 2.0 control-plane crate with dependencies for a TUI dashboard (ratatui, crossterm), async runtime (tokio), SQLite session store (rusqlite), git integration (git2), CLI parsing (clap), serialization (serde), and structured logging (tracing). Release profile enables LTO and binary stripping for minimal production output.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a vendored-openssl feature flag to bundle OpenSSL for cross-platform builds; release profile sets lto=true and codegen-units=1 for maximum optimization.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Alpha-status developer guide for the ECC 2.0 Rust control plane, describing current capabilities (TUI dashboard, SQLite session store, daemon mode, risk-scoring), the intended goals (multi-agent orchestration, worktree management), CLI run and test instructions, and an explicit list of features still missing before GA.

nodes.name: rust-toolchain.toml
nodes.summary: Rust toolchain pin for the ecc2 crate, locking the channel to 1.96 with rustfmt and clippy components. Required because several dependencies use Rust edition 2024 syntax that older toolchains do not support.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Generic project-level CLAUDE.md template demonstrating ECC conventions for code organization, style, testing, and security rules — serves as a canonical starting point for new projects onboarding ECC.

nodes.name: django-api-CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive CLAUDE.md template for Django REST API projects covering Python conventions, Django ORM patterns, DRF serializers, authentication, pytest/Factory Boy testing strategy, and the ECC workflow integration.
nodes.languageNotes: Demonstrates Django-specific patterns: service layer, viewsets, pytest with Factory Boy fixtures, and token-based authentication conventions.

nodes.name: go-microservice-CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: CLAUDE.md template for Go microservices documenting idiomatic Go conventions, repository/service patterns with dependency injection, table-driven tests, and ECC workflow integration.
nodes.languageNotes: Showcases Go-idiomatic patterns: error wrapping with fmt.Errorf, interface-based repositories, table-driven tests, and sqlx for database access.

nodes.name: harmonyos-app-CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Compact CLAUDE.md template for HarmonyOS ArkTS application development covering tech-stack constraints, ArkUI layout rules, DevEco Studio build validation, and Huawei security requirements.
nodes.languageNotes: Targets HarmonyOS NEXT with ArkTS and ArkUI — a Huawei-specific platform with different conventions from Android/iOS.

nodes.name: hud-status-contract.json
nodes.summary: Example HUD status contract document conforming to the ecc.hud-status.v1 schema, demonstrating the full payload shape the ECC status HUD renders — including context window pressure, active agents, todos, checks, cost, risk, queue state, session controls, and Linear/GitHub sync.
nodes.languageNotes: Acts as both a live schema reference and a sample fixture for testing HUD rendering logic; the schema_version field (ecc.hud-status.v1) is the contract identifier.

nodes.name: laravel-api-CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive CLAUDE.md template for Laravel API projects covering PHP conventions, API response envelopes, Eloquent ORM, Sanctum authentication, form request validation, service/controller/policy patterns, queue jobs, and both Pest and PHPUnit test patterns.
nodes.languageNotes: Demonstrates Laravel-specific layered architecture: FormRequest + DTO pipeline, API Resources for response transformation, and Policy-based authorization — the most patterns of any example file.

nodes.name: rails-app-CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive CLAUDE.md template for Ruby on Rails applications covering ActiveRecord conventions, Devise/Pundit authentication, Sidekiq background jobs, Hotwire/ActionCable patterns, Kamal deployment, and RSpec testing with the service object, skinny controller, query object, and background job patterns.
nodes.languageNotes: The largest example file (387 lines); covers Rails-idiomatic patterns including Hotwire (Turbo/Stimulus) for modern frontends alongside traditional backend patterns.

nodes.name: rust-api-CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: CLAUDE.md template for Rust API services built on Axum, covering ownership/borrow checker conventions, SQLx database access, thiserror/anyhow error handling, handler/service/repository layering, integration tests with TestContainers, and the ECC workflow.
nodes.languageNotes: Demonstrates Rust-specific patterns: thiserror for typed errors, anyhow for ad-hoc contexts, SQLx compile-time query checking, and TestContainers for hermetic integration tests.

nodes.name: saas-nextjs-CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: CLAUDE.md template for Next.js SaaS applications with Prisma, Clerk authentication, and Stripe billing — covering server action patterns, API response format, E2E flows for critical SaaS paths, and ECC workflow integration.
nodes.languageNotes: Targets Next.js App Router server actions as the primary data-mutation pattern, with Clerk for auth and Stripe for billing as canonical SaaS integrations.

nodes.name: statusline.json
nodes.summary: Configuration snippet showing how to wire the ECC statusline script as a Claude Code statusLine command, providing model, task, cost, tool count, file count, duration, directory, and context-pressure bar in the IDE status area.
nodes.languageNotes: The statusLine.command field references ecc-statusline.js which reads metrics from the ecc-metrics-bridge.js PostToolUse hook; both must be installed for full display.

nodes.name: user-CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Example user-level (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) configuration demonstrating how to set global personal preferences, modular rules references, available agents, privacy constraints, code style, git conventions, and knowledge-capture workflows across all projects.

nodes.name: install-components.json
nodes.summary: Defines the full catalog of installable ECC components (baseline, language, framework, capability, agent, and skill families), each mapping to one or more module IDs. Acts as the user-facing component registry that the installer resolves against install-modules.json.

nodes.name: install-modules.json
nodes.summary: Declares every installable ECC module with its kind, file paths, harness targets, dependencies, cost, stability, and default-install flag. This is the ground-truth module registry that installation tooling reads to copy or link the correct files for each selected module.
nodes.languageNotes: Each module entry carries a targets array listing the AI harness platforms it supports (claude, cursor, opencode, codex, zed, etc.) and an explicit dependency list enabling topological resolution during install.

nodes.name: install-profiles.json

nodes.languageNotes: The 'full' profile enumerates all currently classified modules, making it the canonical reference for the complete ECC feature surface; guardrails docs note 'full' as the recommended install profile.

nodes.name: coding-style.md

nodes.languageNotes: Emphasizes Angular's modern standalone component model, signal-based state, and functional DI patterns (inject() function, InjectionToken) over class-based approaches.

nodes.name: ecc-install-config.schema.json
nodes.summary: JSON Schema (draft-07) defining the structure of the ECC install configuration file, including target platform enum (claude, cursor, codex, etc.), profile, module lists, and include/exclude component filters.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses draft-07 strict schema with additionalProperties:false and an enum of 11 supported target platforms.

nodes.name: hooks.schema.json
nodes.summary: JSON Schema defining the full Claude Code hooks configuration format, covering all hook event types (SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, etc.) and three hook action types: command, http, and prompt/agent.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses $defs for reusable hookItem, matcherEntry, and stringArray types; supports both object and array root forms via oneOf.

nodes.name: install-components.schema.json
nodes.summary: JSON Schema for the ECC install components manifest, describing the array of installable component definitions each with an id (namespaced by family), family enum, description, and list of module ids.
nodes.languageNotes: Component id must match pattern ^(baseline|lang|framework|capability|agent|skill|locale):[a-z0-9-]+$.

nodes.name: install-modules.schema.json
nodes.summary: JSON Schema for the ECC install modules manifest, specifying each module's kind (rules, agents, commands, hooks, etc.), file paths, supported targets, dependencies, cost (light/medium/heavy), and stability (experimental/beta/stable).
nodes.languageNotes: Modules carry target allowlists (11 platforms), cost and stability enums, and a defaultInstall boolean for profile resolution.

nodes.name: install-profiles.schema.json
nodes.summary: JSON Schema for ECC install profiles, where each named profile (e.g. 'full', 'minimal') maps to a description and a list of module ids to install.

nodes.name: install-state.schema.json
nodes.summary: JSON Schema for the ECC installation state record written after an install run, capturing target details, the original request (profile, modules, include/exclude), the resolved module selection, source provenance (repoVersion, repoCommit), and the full list of file operations performed.
nodes.languageNotes: Schema version locked to const 'ecc.install.v1'; operations array uses additionalProperties:true for forward compatibility.

nodes.name: package-manager.schema.json
nodes.summary: Minimal JSON Schema for the persisted package manager preference, constraining the value to npm, pnpm, yarn, or bun with an optional ISO 8601 timestamp of when the preference was last set.

nodes.name: plugin.schema.json
nodes.summary: JSON Schema for a Claude Code plugin manifest (plugin.json), validating fields like name, semver version, author, skills, commands, mcpServers, and a features summary block counting agents/skills/commands and listing hook events.
nodes.languageNotes: Author field supports both a plain string and a structured object with name+url; mcpServers accepts string, array, or object for flexibility.

nodes.name: provenance.schema.json
nodes.summary: JSON Schema for skill provenance metadata attached to learned and imported skills in ~/.claude/skills/, requiring source origin, creation timestamp, confidence score (0-1), and author.

nodes.name: state-store.schema.json
nodes.summary: Comprehensive JSON Schema ($id: ecc.state-store.v1) for the ECC runtime state store, defining entity shapes for sessions, skillRuns, skillVersions, decisions, installState, governanceEvents, and workItems — the full persistence surface of the harness.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses $defs for shared primitive types (nonEmptyString, nullableString, nullableInteger, jsonValue) referenced throughout all entity definitions.

nodes.name: gan-harness.sh
nodes.summary: GAN-style generator-evaluator loop orchestrator that runs iterative Claude agent cycles (planner → generator → evaluator) against a scoring rubric, halting when a weighted score threshold is met or a plateau is detected.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the generator-adversarial-network pattern as a shell harness: each cycle invokes `claude -p` for three distinct agent roles, writes feedback files, extracts scores via regex, and applies awk-based floating-point comparison for pass/plateau logic.

nodes.name: extract_score
nodes.summary: Extracts the weighted TOTAL score from an evaluator feedback file using multiple regex fallback patterns against common markdown table and verdict formats.

nodes.name: orchestrate-codex-worker.sh
nodes.summary: Runs a single Codex CLI worker agent inside a git worktree, writing structured status and handoff markdown files to communicate task state back to the swarm orchestrator.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps `codex exec` with task/handoff/status file contracts; the three-argument interface (task-file, handoff-file, status-file) is the swarm coordination protocol.

nodes.name: write_status
nodes.summary: Writes a structured markdown status file capturing current state, timestamp, branch, and worktree path for the orchestrator to poll.

nodes.name: release.sh
nodes.summary: Version-bump release script that atomically updates all versioned manifests across package.json, plugin.json, AGENTS.md, agent.yaml, README files, and marketplace configs before staging, tagging, and pushing a release commit.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses macOS/Linux portability guards for `sed -i`, delegates complex JSON mutation to inline `node -e` snippets, and validates semver format via bash regex before touching any file.

nodes.name: update_package_lock_version
nodes.summary: Updates both the top-level `version` and `packages[""].version` fields in a package-lock.json file via an inline Node.js script with strict validation.

nodes.name: update_readme_version_row
nodes.summary: Finds and replaces a semver token in a specific markdown table row identified by label and three column values, failing if the row cannot be located.

nodes.name: update_latest_release_heading
nodes.summary: Updates the `### vX.Y.Z` heading in a markdown file's latest release section to the new version, with error detection if the pattern is not found.

nodes.name: update_selective_install_repo_version
nodes.summary: Updates the `repoVersion` example value in the selective-install architecture documentation to the new semver string.

nodes.name: update_agents_version
nodes.summary: Updates a labeled version line (e.g. `**Version:** X.Y.Z`) in an AGENTS.md file, supporting different label strings for multilingual docs.

nodes.name: update_agent_yaml_version
nodes.summary: Updates the `version:` key in agent.yaml to the new semver string via an inline Node.js regex replacement.

nodes.name: update_codex_marketplace_version
nodes.summary: Parses the Codex marketplace JSON, locates the `ecc` plugin entry, sets its version field, and writes the file back with pretty-printing.

nodes.name: update_opencode_hook_banner_version
nodes.summary: Updates the version string embedded in the OpenCode ECC hooks plugin banner comment to keep the displayed version in sync with the release.

nodes.name: sync-ecc-to-codex.sh
nodes.summary: Syncs ECC assets (AGENTS.md, MCP config, commands, git hooks, agent role files, rule-pack prompts) into a local Codex CLI installation, merging marker-delimited ECC blocks while preserving user content and supporting a dry-run mode.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses marker-based AGENTS.md merging (BEGIN/END ECC markers) with three-branch awk logic to handle clean, malformed, and missing marker states; delegates TOML and MCP merging to companion Node.js scripts.

nodes.name: remove_section_inplace
nodes.summary: Removes a named TOML section and all its key-value lines from a file in-place using awk, writing through a temp file to preserve symlinks.

nodes.name: extract_toml_value
nodes.summary: Reads a quoted string value from a specific section and key in a TOML file using awk without any external TOML parser dependency.

nodes.name: extract_context7_key
nodes.summary: Extracts a Context7 MCP API key value from a config file by matching the `--key` argument pattern, using an inline Node.js heredoc for the parsing logic.

nodes.name: generate_prompt_file
nodes.summary: Transforms an ECC command markdown file into a Codex prompt file by prepending a standard header and stripping the YAML frontmatter via awk.

nodes.name: write_extension_prompt
nodes.summary: Writes a named extension prompt file to the Codex prompts destination, respecting dry-run mode and appending the filename to the extension manifest.

nodes.name: linked-signal.md

nodes.name: loading-strategies.md

nodes.name: route-guards.md

nodes.summary: Reference guide for testing Angular routing using RouterTestingHarness, covering setup, example configurations, key concepts, writing navigation tests, and best practices for router unit tests.

nodes.name: show-routes-with-outlets.md

nodes.name: observer-loop.sh
nodes.summary: Background daemon loop for the continuous-learning observer: polls on a configurable interval, fires on SIGUSR1, enforces analysis cooldowns and re-entrancy guards, spawns an ephemeral Claude Haiku subprocess to detect patterns and write instinct files, then archives processed observations.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses SIGUSR1 for out-of-band trigger; re-entrancy is guarded via ANALYZING/PENDING_ANALYSIS shell vars. Includes a watchdog subshell with timeout to kill runaway Claude child processes.

nodes.name: file_mtime_epoch
nodes.summary: Returns a file's modification time as a Unix epoch integer, with portable fallback between GNU stat (-c) and BSD stat (-f).

nodes.name: latest_activity_epoch
nodes.summary: Returns the most recent of the observations file's or activity file's modification epoch, used to determine how long the observer has been idle.

nodes.name: exit_if_idle_without_sessions
nodes.summary: Terminates the observer process if there are no active session leases and the project has been idle beyond the configured timeout.

nodes.name: wait_for_claude_analysis
nodes.summary: Waits for a Claude child process to complete, re-waiting when SIGUSR1 interrupts the wait syscall so signals do not cause false child failure reports.

nodes.name: analyze_observations
nodes.summary: Core analysis routine: samples recent observations via tail, builds a structured prompt instructing Claude Haiku to detect patterns and write instinct files, spawns Claude with a watchdog timeout, and archives processed observations on success.

nodes.name: on_usr1
nodes.summary: SIGUSR1 signal handler: wakes the sleeping loop, enforces the analysis cooldown, and defers the trigger when analysis is already in progress.

nodes.name: observer.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification and prompt guide for the background observer: defines when to run, input format, four pattern-detection categories (corrections, error resolutions, repeated workflows, tool preferences), output instinct format, scope decision heuristics, confidence scoring, and promotion rules from project-scoped to global instincts.

nodes.name: session-guardian.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-analysis gate script called by observer-loop.sh before each Claude session: enforces three ordered checks (active-hours time window, per-project cooldown log with mkdir locking, and user-idle detection) and exits 0 to proceed or 1 to skip the cycle.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses mkdir as an atomic lock primitive for concurrent-safe cooldown log updates; supports overnight active-hour windows via HHMM integer comparison.

nodes.name: get_idle_seconds
nodes.summary: Cross-platform user-idle detection: reads HID idle time on macOS via ioreg, uses xprintidle on Linux, queries Win32 GetLastInputInfo via PowerShell on Windows, and fails open (returns 0) on unknown platforms.

nodes.name: start-observer.sh
nodes.summary: Launcher and lifecycle manager for the background observer daemon: reads config, detects the current project, and supports start/stop/status/--reset subcommands; on start it launches observer-loop.sh via nohup and scans early log output for confirmation-prompt patterns before confirming successful startup.

nodes.name: stop_observer_if_running
nodes.summary: Reads the project-scoped PID file and sends SIGTERM to the observer process if it is still alive, cleaning up stale PID files when the process is not found.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Deprecated v1 continuous-learning skill documentation kept for archival reference, describing the Stop-hook session-evaluation pattern and comparing it with the v2 instinct-based approach in continuous-learning-v2.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Runtime configuration for the v1 continuous-learning skill, controlling session length thresholds, extraction sensitivity, learned-skills output path, and which pattern types to detect or ignore.

nodes.name: evaluate-session.sh
nodes.summary: Stop-hook shell script that reads Claude Code session transcript JSON from stdin, checks whether the session meets the minimum message count threshold, and emits a signal for Claude to extract reusable patterns and save them to the learned-skills directory.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses jq for JSON config parsing with a graceful fallback to hardcoded defaults when jq is unavailable; reads transcript path from hook stdin JSON with a legacy env-var fallback.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Orchestration spec for the frontend-slides ECC skill, defining a 7-step workflow (detect mode, discover content/style, build, enforce viewport fit, validate, deliver) with non-negotiables, anti-patterns, and a deliverable checklist for generating HTML presentation slides.

nodes.name: STYLE_PRESETS.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive style preset catalog for the frontend-slides skill, providing 12 named visual themes (Bold Signal, Electric Studio, Neon Cyber, etc.) with mandatory viewport-fit CSS, mood-to-preset mappings, animation feel mappings, and anti-pattern guidance.
nodes.languageNotes: Embeds complete CSS code blocks for each preset alongside the mandatory base CSS that must be included in every presentation.

nodes.name: animation-patterns.md
nodes.summary: CSS animation pattern reference for the frontend-slides skill, cataloging entrance animations, background effects, and interactive effects with effect-to-feeling mappings and troubleshooting guidance for overflow and viewport issues.

nodes.name: html-template.md
nodes.summary: Reference HTML boilerplate template for the frontend-slides skill, covering base HTML structure, required JavaScript features (slide navigation, keyboard/touch/gesture controls), optional inline editing, image pipeline (skip-if-no-images pattern), and file structure guidance.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains complete inline HTML/CSS/JS code examples for a full single-file presentation, including touch gesture handling and dynamic viewport height (dvh) usage.

nodes.name: viewport-base.css
nodes.summary: Mandatory CSS base file for the frontend-slides skill that enforces strict viewport fitting for every presentation slide, using scroll-snap, CSS custom properties with clamp() for responsive typography, and media queries for short/narrow viewports and reduced-motion accessibility.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CSS scroll-snap-type, 100dvh (dynamic viewport height for mobile), and clamp() throughout — all are required for reliable viewport-fit across device sizes.

nodes.name: enrichment-agent.md

nodes.name: outreach-drafter.md

nodes.name: signal-scorer.md

nodes.name: can-decode.md
nodes.summary: Documents the canDecode() utility for checking browser codec support before rendering video, including usage patterns with Blob and URL sources.

nodes.name: charts.md

nodes.name: images.md
nodes.summary: Explains the Remotion Img component's usage, restrictions, sizing and positioning, dynamic path patterns, and how to retrieve image dimensions at runtime.

nodes.name: import-srt-captions.md
nodes.summary: Guides importing .srt subtitle files into Remotion using @remotion/captions parseSrt(), converting subtitle entries to a usable captions data structure for rendering.

nodes.name: lottie.md
nodes.summary: Documents using Lottie animations inside Remotion via @remotion/lottie, covering the LottieAnimationData type, the Lottie component, and frame-driven playback control.

nodes.name: measuring-dom-nodes.md
nodes.summary: Short guide on using React refs and getBoundingClientRect() to measure DOM element dimensions within a Remotion composition for layout calculations.

nodes.name: measuring-text.md
nodes.summary: Reference guide for @remotion/layout-utils text measurement APIs, covering measureText(), fitText(), and overflow checking with best practices for font loading and caching.

nodes.name: sequencing.md
nodes.summary: Rule document covering Remotion sequencing patterns including `<Sequence>`, `<Series>`, premounting, nested sequences, and frame-of-reference behavior inside sequences.

nodes.name: tailwind.md
nodes.summary: Short rule document directing use of TailwindCSS in Remotion projects while prohibiting CSS transition/animate classes in favor of `useCurrentFrame()`-driven animation.

nodes.name: text-animations.md
nodes.summary: Rule document describing typography and text animation patterns in Remotion, covering typewriter effects (string-slice approach) and word-highlight animations with asset references.

nodes.summary: Comprehensive rule document covering Remotion interpolation and animation timing, including linear interpolation with clamping, spring animation physical properties, delay/duration patterns, combining springs, and easing functions.

nodes.summary: Rule document listing three Remotion audio-to-caption transcription options: local Whisper.cpp, browser-based whisper-web (WASM), and cloud OpenAI Whisper API, with trade-offs.

nodes.summary: Rule document covering fullscreen scene transitions in Remotion using `<TransitionSeries>`, including available transition types (fade, slide, wipe, flip), timing options, and duration calculation patterns.

nodes.name: trimming.md
nodes.summary: Rule document explaining Remotion animation trimming patterns using `<Sequence>` with negative `from` values to trim the start, `durationInFrames` to trim the end, and nested sequences to combine trim and delay.

nodes.name: videos.md

nodes.name: architecture.md

Intended as lookup material for the tinystruct-patterns skill.

nodes.name: data-handling.md

nodes.name: system-usage.md
nodes.summary: Reference for tinystruct runtime system features including Context/CLI argument access, pluggable session management (Memory and Redis), Server-Sent Events via SSEPushManager, file uploads via FileEntity, outbound HTTP via URLRequest/HTTPHandler, and the event dispatcher system.

nodes.name: testing.md
nodes.summary: Reference for JUnit 5 testing patterns in tinystruct applications, covering unit test setup via ActionRegistry singleton initialization, invoke-based action testing, ActionRegistry routing match tests, and HTTP integration testing using the built-in HttpServer with URLRequest/HTTPHandler.

nodes.name: api-reference.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive VideoDB Python SDK API reference covering Connection, Collection, Video, Audio, Image, Timeline, SearchResult, Meeting, and RTStream/Capture objects, including all methods, properties, parameters, enums, and exception types.

nodes.name: VERSION
nodes.summary: Single-line version file containing the current release version string for the ECC plugin.

layers.name: Skills Catalog
layers.description: The 271 bundled skill specs (SKILL.md) and their supporting assets — parser scripts, templates, and per-skill JSON/config — that define every reusable capability ECC exposes to Claude Code agents.

layers.name: Agents & Commands
layers.description: Spec-driven Markdown definitions for all 67 named agents, 92 slash commands, 3 context presets, and 13 backward-compatibility command shims that form ECC's agentic dispatch surface.

layers.name: Coding Rules
layers.description: Per-language and cross-cutting Markdown coding-standard rules (Python, Rust, TypeScript, React, etc.) that ECC injects into agent sessions to enforce consistent development practices.

layers.name: Automation Scripts
layers.description: Node.js operational backbone — the install pipeline (lib/), lifecycle hook dispatchers (hooks/), CI helpers (ci/), Codex tooling, Discord bots, and the hook configuration manifests that wire ECC's event-driven behaviors.

layers.name: Rust Control Plane
layers.description: The ecc2 Rust binary that provides a TUI dashboard, session management daemon, inter-process communication, observability, and git worktree control — the high-performance native layer of the ECC stack.

layers.name: LLM & Integration Layer
layers.description: Python provider-agnostic LLM abstraction (claude, openai, ollama, astraflow) with prompt builder and CLI selector, plus the Aura integration adapter — the AI inference plumbing underlying ECC's intelligence features.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Jest and shell-based test suite covering lifecycle hooks, install library functions, CI scripts, and integration scenarios — the primary quality gate for ECC's Node.js automation layer.

layers.name: Plugin Manifest & Configuration
layers.description: The Claude Code plugin manifests (plugin.json, marketplace.json), JSON schemas for install components and profiles, MCP server configs, Cursor scaffolds, evaluator-rag prototype fixtures, example CLAUDE.md templates, and all root-level configuration files that define and govern ECC's deployable structure.

layers.name: CI/CD & Project Documentation
layers.description: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines (release, test, SLSA provenance, supply-chain watch), GitHub project config (dependabot, funding, PR templates, Copilot prompts), and all root-level Markdown documentation (README, CHANGELOG, CONTRIBUTING, guides, SOUL) plus the research archive.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: The README is the authoritative map of the entire system: it explains ECC's identity as a cross-harness agent operating system, enumerates the seven major component kinds (Skills, Agents, Commands, Rules, Hooks, Automation, Rust/Python layers), and describes the three-tier install model (modules → components → profiles). Reading it first establishes the vocabulary used throughout every other file in the tour. The companion SOUL.md distills ECC's philosophical core — the principles and agent-orchestration philosophy that explain why the architecture is shaped the way it is.
tour.languageLesson: ECC is a markdown-dominant codebase: Skills, Agents, Commands, and Rules are all spec-driven Markdown files rather than executable code. The plugin runtime reads these specs and injects them into agent context, so the 'source of truth' for most functionality is a SKILL.md or command .md, not a .js or .py file.

tour.title: Plugin Manifest & npm Package
tour.description: Two config files define ECC as an installable artifact. The plugin.json is the Claude Code plugin manifest: it declares plugin identity, the directories that contain skills and commands, hook registrations, and settings schema — it is what Claude Code reads when a user installs ECC via `/install ecc@ecc`. The package.json alongside it is the npm manifest for `ecc-universal`, listing every file that ships to npm and the scripts that drive the CI/CD pipeline. Together they answer the question: how does ECC get from a git repo into a running agent session?
tour.languageLesson: Claude Code plugins use a plugin.json manifest to declare capabilities without any code: a skills directory path, a commands path, and hook entry points are all that's needed to extend the harness. This manifest-first design means ECC's 271 skills and 92 commands are registered purely through directory conventions.

tour.title: Skills Catalog — the Dominant Surface
tour.description: Skills are the largest component kind in ECC (271 files) and the primary interface users interact with. CLAUDE.md at the repo root is the in-session instruction file that describes which skills exist and how to invoke them; AGENTS.md expands on this for the 67 named subagent definitions; and CONTRIBUTING.md shows exactly how a new skill is added — the template convention, required frontmatter, and wiring into plugin.json. This trio covers the full lifecycle of a skill: what it is, how it runs, and how to extend the catalog.
tour.languageLesson: ECC skills follow a spec-driven pattern: each SKILL.md describes a capability in natural language with a structured preamble. The harness reads this file and injects it into agent context at invocation time — there is no compilation step. This makes skills auditable, diffable, and writable by non-engineers.

tour.title: Commands & Rules
tour.description: Beyond skills, ECC exposes 92 slash commands (COMMANDS-QUICK-REF.md) and 114 per-language coding-standard rules (RULES.md). Commands give users imperative actions (/plan, /review, /ship, etc.) while rules inject language-specific constraints (Python style, Rust idioms, TypeScript patterns) into every agent session. These two layers together shape the agent's behavior at the command surface and at the code-generation level — they are the 'policy layer' of the OS.

tour.title: Install Pipeline — Core Scripts
tour.description: The Node.js automation layer (scripts/) is ECC's operational backbone. The fan-in analysis identifies `utils.js` (24 inbound edges) as the most-depended-upon file in the entire codebase — it provides path resolution, file I/O, git helpers, and process execution used by every other script. `install-manifests.js` (16 inbound edges) is the manifest loader that reads ECC's three-tier install model (modules/components/profiles) from disk. `install-executor.js` is the engine that materializes an install plan across all supported targets (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Antigravity). These three files form the core of the install pipeline.
tour.languageLesson: The install pipeline uses a plan-then-execute pattern: `install-manifests.js` resolves what needs to be installed from declarative YAML manifests, `install-executor.js` turns that plan into file-system operations across multiple harness targets. This separation makes the install logic testable in isolation from file-system side effects.

tour.title: Install Targets & Lifecycle
tour.description: Building on the core engine from Step 5, `install-targets/registry.js` (the highest fan-out file in the codebase at 12 outbound edges) is the central registry of every harness target ECC can install into — Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and more. Each adapter knows how to write config files, symlinks, and manifests for its specific target. `install-lifecycle.js` wraps the whole pipeline in discovery, health-check, repair, and uninstall operations, persisting state via `install-state.js`. This is how ECC becomes idempotent and self-healing.

tour.title: Lifecycle Hooks
tour.description: Hooks are ECC's event-driven behavior layer — they run at session start, before/after bash commands, and before/after tool use. The `bash-hook-dispatcher.js` (highest fan-out hook file, 11 outbound edges) is the central router that composes individual hook handlers into a pipeline for PreBash and PostBash events. `session-start.js` injects rich context (recent session history, learned skills, MCP inventory) into every new Claude session. `pretooluse-visible-output.js` provides the shared utility that lets hooks attach `additionalContext` to tool calls. Together these three files implement ECC's 'always-on intelligence' — the ambient enhancements every session gets for free.
tour.languageLesson: Claude Code hooks receive structured JSON on stdin and must respond with JSON on stdout. ECC's dispatcher pattern composes multiple handlers — each a pure function of the hook payload — into a single hook entry point, keeping individual handlers small and independently testable.

tour.title: Session Adapters & MCP Inventory
tour.description: Two tightly coupled clusters (identified by the topology analysis) handle cross-harness session data. The session adapters cluster (`canonical-session.js`, `registry.js`, and harness-specific adapters) normalizes session history from Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and tmux into a single canonical schema — enabling ECC to inject cross-harness context regardless of which tool the user is running. The MCP inventory cluster (`canonical-mcp.js`, `collect.js`, harness readers) performs the same normalization for MCP server configurations. Both clusters follow the same registry-and-adapter pattern as the install targets.

tour.title: Rust Control Plane (ecc2/)
tour.description: The `ecc2/` directory is ECC's high-performance native layer, a Rust binary named `ecc-tui`. `main.rs` is the CLI entry point implementing the full control-plane surface via clap subcommands: session management, schedules, remote dispatch, worktree control, memory graph, and observability export. `config/mod.rs` (9 inbound edges — one of the most-imported files in the Rust layer) defines all runtime config structs that drive agent behavior. `tui/dashboard.rs` implements the interactive terminal dashboard. `session/store.rs` provides the SQLite-backed persistence layer. The Cargo.toml manifest defines this as the `ecc-tui` crate with TUI and async-runtime dependencies.
tour.languageLesson: Rust's module system maps directly to the filesystem: `config/mod.rs` is the public interface for the entire `config` module. The `ecc2` crate uses Tokio for async I/O and Ratatui for the terminal UI — two idioms common in Rust CLI tools where performance and low latency matter.

tour.title: Python LLM Abstraction Layer (src/)
tour.description: The `src/llm/` package is ECC's provider-agnostic AI inference layer. `core/types.py` (9 inbound edges — the most-imported Python file) defines the canonical type system: Role, ProviderType, message and tool-call dataclasses. `core/interface.py` defines the abstract `LLMProvider` base class and the full error hierarchy, establishing the contract every provider must implement. `providers/resolver.py` reads environment variables to instantiate the correct provider at runtime (Claude, OpenAI, Ollama, astraflow). The `__main__.py` entry point enables `python -m llm` invocation from CI scripts and hooks.
tour.languageLesson: The abstract base class + resolver pattern is the standard Python approach to provider switching without if-chains: `interface.py` defines the contract, each provider in `providers/` implements it, and `resolver.py` picks the right one at runtime from environment config. Adding a new LLM provider requires only a new class and a line in the resolver.

tour.title: CI/CD & Supply-Chain Security
tour.description: ECC's GitHub Actions pipelines close the loop from code to shipped artifact. `ci.yml` runs the full test suite across a 3×3×4 OS/Node/package-manager matrix — the broadest compatibility gate in the repo. `release.yml` handles tag-triggered publishing to npm including an IOC (indicators of compromise) supply-chain scan before any artifact is published. `generator-generic-ossf-slsa3-publish.yml` generates SLSA level-3 provenance for every release, providing a cryptographically verifiable build chain. `supply-chain-watch.yml` runs every 6 hours to continuously verify npm registry signatures. This pipeline reflects ECC's identity as infrastructure software where supply-chain integrity is non-negotiable.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions reusable workflows (`workflow_call`) let ECC factor the test and release logic into `reusable-test.yml` and `reusable-release.yml`, then call them from both CI and release pipelines without duplication. SLSA provenance is generated as a separate workflow that triggers on `release: published` — decoupling build verification from the release process itself.
